name: Air Swim
description: Swim through the air as if it were water. Aquatic Morphus Nightbane suffer no drying-out or air-breathing penalties while using this Talent. Speed equals the Swimming skill.

enhancement.summary: 2 P.P.E. per each minute after the first minute.

ppe.notes: 5 P.P.E. for the first minute; 2 P.P.E. per each minute thereafter.

limitations.otherLimitations: Maximum height above the ground is 15 feet (4.6 m) per level of experience.

ranges.summary: Self; altitude capped at 15 feet (4.6 m) per level above ground.

duration.summary: 5 P.P.E. for the first minute; 2 P.P.E. per each additional minute.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — swim through air at Swimming skill speed; maximum altitude 15 feet (4.6 m) per level above ground.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Swim through the air as if it were water at Swimming skill speed.
mobilityBlocks.notes: Maximum height above ground: 15 feet (4.6 m) per level of experience.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Aquatic Morphus Nightbane suffer no drying-out or air-breathing penalties while Air Swim is active.

combatMechanics.notes: Movement only — no direct attack. Speed equals the character's Swimming skill percentage.

statusAlterations.summary: Swims through open air as if traversing water.

name: Air Grab
description: Grabs a target at range with a gesture — holds for two melee actions (~5 seconds); the captive cannot move or physically attack. May squeeze (supernatural P.S. punch damage using the Nightbane's P.S. −5, counts as two melee attacks), pull the victim to the user in one melee action, or throw up to 10 feet (3 m) away (failed fall/impact: 1D6 S.D.C., lose two melee actions and initiative). Break free or resist pull with one melee action if P.S. equals or exceeds the Nightbane's.

enhancement.summary: 8 P.P.E. per grabbing action or attack.

ppe.notes: Each grab, squeeze, pull, or throw costs 8 P.P.E.

limitations.otherLimitations: Target must know they are being targeted to attempt avoidance; dodge requires 16 or higher.

ranges.summary: 50 feet (15.2 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Hold: two melee actions (~5 seconds). Pull: one melee action (~2 seconds).

save.summary: Victims who know they are targeted may dodge at 16 or higher to avoid the grab.
save.effectsOnSuccess: Grab avoided; P.P.E. is still spent.
save.notes: Avoidance is only possible if the target knows they are being targeted.

rangeMechanics.summary: 50 feet (15.2 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Grabs a target at range with a gesture. Holds for two melee actions (~5 seconds); captive cannot move or physically attack. 8 P.P.E.
Supernatural P.S. punch damage using the Nightbane's P.S. minus 5; counts as two melee attacks. 8 P.P.E.
Pulls a grabbed victim to the user in one melee action (~2 seconds). 8 P.P.E.
Throws a grabbed victim up to 10 feet (3 m) away. Failed roll with fall/impact: 1D6 S.D.C., lose two melee actions and initiative. 8 P.P.E.

holdsAndEscapes.summary: Captive cannot move or physically attack while held. Break free or resist pull with one melee action if P.S. equals or exceeds the Nightbane's.
holdsAndEscapes.combinedPsToBreakEffect: equal to or greater than the Nightbane's
holdsAndEscapes.holdDurationSummary: Two melee actions (~5 seconds) for a standard grab.

attacksAndDamage.summary: Air squeeze uses supernatural P.S. punch damage at the Nightbane's P.S. minus 5 (two melee attacks). Throw inflicts 1D6 S.D.C. on a failed roll with fall/impact.

damage.summary: Air squeeze: supernatural P.S. punch damage using the Nightbane's P.S. minus 5 (two melee attacks).
Throw: 1D6 S.D.C. on failed roll with fall/impact.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Target is held, squeezed, pulled, or thrown per the chosen mode.
savingThrow.notes: Dodge only if the target knows they are being targeted.

statusEffects.summary: Held captive cannot move or physically attack for the hold duration.
Failed throw impact: lose two melee actions and initiative.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: While held: cannot move or physically attack. Failed throw: lose two melee actions and initiative.

statusEffects.name: Air Grab Hold
statusEffects.summary: Cannot move or physically attack while held.

name: All-Nighter
description: Staves off the effects of sleep deprivation for an entire day at a time, enabling the user to remain fully awake and completely alert without sleep. Useful on guard duty, when on the run from authorities, or when maintaining a dual identity as an average citizen and resistance fighter. Grants +1 to Perception and +3 to save against sedatives or sleep-inducing powers.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. per use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only delays the need for sleep; the character must eventually catch up with at least four hours of rest per day skipped. Can only be activated once per day and may not be used on an unwilling subject. Going more than 3–5 days without sleep feels uncomfortable; after 7 consecutive days awake, roll once per day on the Random Insanity Table (Nightbane RPG, p. 44). Insanities are temporary and disappear after sufficient rest.

ranges.summary: Self or one other by touch.

duration.summary: 24 hours.

notes: Nightbane still need a relatively normal amount of sleep; this Talent postpones deprivation rather than replacing rest.

activationRequirements.summary: Once per day; willing subject only (not usable on unwilling targets).

statusAlterations.summary: Staves off sleep-deprivation effects; user remains fully awake and completely alert without sleep for the duration.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Must catch up at least four hours of rest per day of sleep skipped. Feels uncomfortable after 3–5 days without sleep. After 7 consecutive days awake, roll once per day on the Random Insanity Table (Nightbane RPG, p. 44); insanities are temporary and clear after sufficient rest.

name: Anti-Arcane
description: When activating this Talent, the Nightbane is surrounded by an aura of black light (easily confused with the Shadow Shield). While this aura is in effect, the Nightbane is utterly invulnerable to the effects of magic! Any magical spell that can be resisted by a save against magic will have no effect on the character. Spells that must be dodged, or have no saving throw, will work normally.

duration.summary: 1 minute (4 melees); must be reactivated when it expires.

notes: Spells resisted by a save against magic have no effect while the aura is active. Spells that must be dodged or allow no saving throw work normally.

activationTime.summary: Short concentration (a few seconds, equivalent to one melee attack/action), followed by P.P.E. expenditure.

rules: Magical spells that can be resisted by a save against magic have no effect while the aura is active.
Spells that must be dodged, or that allow no saving throw, work normally.

combatMechanics.notes: Protective Talent — typically combinable with other Talents unless otherwise noted. Utterly invulnerable to magic effects that allow a save vs magic; dodge-only and no-save spells are unaffected.

statusAlterations.summary: Surrounded by an aura of black light — easily confused with Shadow Shield.

name: Ashes to Ashes
description: The Nightbane must be holding or touching a combustible object to sacrifice, reducing it to ashes in a flash of flames. In the same instant, that fire is directed at a chosen target (line of sight required). Damage depends on the weight category of the sacrificed object.

activationTiers.summary: Category 1 (1–2 lbs / 0.45–0.90 kg): 2 P.P.E., 1D6 damage
Category 2 (3–5 lbs / 1.3–2.25 kg): 2 P.P.E., 2D6 damage
Category 3 (6–20 lbs / 2.7–9 kg): 2 P.P.E., 4D6 damage
Category 4 (25–35 lbs / 11–16 kg): 2 P.P.E., 1D4×10 damage
Category 5 (40–80 lbs / 18–36 kg): 2 P.P.E., 2D4×10 damage
Category 6 (85–120 lbs / 38–54 kg): 2 P.P.E., 2D6×10 damage

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per weight category of the sacrificed object.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only a single combustible object (generally nothing larger than a love seat) may be sacrificed per use — not a house, porch, fence section, or wall portion. Combustible materials only: wood, paper, rubber, and fabric. Cannot use ceramic, metal, plastic, stone, concrete, magic items, living plants, or living beings. Whole objects only; portions cannot be sacrificed. Damage applies to armor or magical shields first. Fire-resistant targets take half damage; fire-impervious targets take no damage (though clothing may burn). Line of sight required to the attack target.

ranges.summary: Touch for the sacrificed object; 100 feet (30.5 m) for the attack on the target.

duration.summary: Instant.

save.summary: Living targets save vs magic (15+). Failure: full damage. Success: 20% damage (round down). No dodge — the target is suddenly consumed in flames.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch to sacrifice the combustible object; 100 feet (30.5 m) line-of-sight attack on the chosen target.

damage.summary: 1D6 (1–2 lbs / 0.45–0.90 kg): heavy books, axe handle, broom, jeans, pillow, and similar
2D6 (3–5 lbs / 1.3–2.25 kg): large heavy books, baseball bat, staff, large picture frame, and similar
4D6 (6–20 lbs / 2.7–9 kg): very large books, small shelving, light table, wooden chair, split log, and similar
1D4×10 (25–35 lbs / 11–16 kg): hollow-core door, small coffee table, desk chair, folding table, wooden chest, and similar
2D4×10 (40–80 lbs / 18–36 kg): pallet, bookshelf, kitchen table, solid-core door, armchair, large log, and similar
2D6×10 (85–120 lbs / 38–54 kg): heavy wood table, picnic bench, large desk, large dead branch or log, and similar

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Full damage.

combatMechanics.notes: No dodge. Non-living targets (objects, structures) take full damage with no save and need not be flammable. Damage applies to armor or magical shields first. Fire-resistant targets take half damage; fire-impervious targets take no damage (clothing and personal belongings may still burn).

damageModifiersNote: Fire-resistant targets take half damage; fire-impervious targets take no damage.

name: Astral Self
description: Becomes a full astral being with no physical body — astral projection abilities and limitations (Nightbane RPG p. 70) without a silver cord or a body left behind. Astral S.D.C. equals combined S.D.C. and Hit Points × 2. Can enter the Astral Plane, affect insubstantial and astral beings, and visit astral domains. May remain in astral form indefinitely.

ppe.notes: 8 P.P.E. to activate; 8 P.P.E. to return to physical form (must be in the physical world — Earth or the Nightlands).

limitations.otherLimitations: Returning to physical form requires being in the physical world (Earth or the Nightlands), not the Astral Plane.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Indefinite while in astral form; 8 P.P.E. to return to physical form.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — full astral transformation with no physical body left behind.

activationRequirements.summary: Returning to physical form requires being in the physical world (Earth or the Nightlands), not the Astral Plane.

temporaryResourcePools.summary: Astral S.D.C. equals combined physical S.D.C. and Hit Points × 2.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Gains all abilities and limitations of astral projection (Nightbane RPG, p. 70) without a silver cord or a body left behind. Can enter the Astral Plane, affect insubstantial and astral beings, and visit astral domains or kingdoms.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Astral travel per Nightbane RPG astral projection rules — can enter the Astral Plane and visit astral domains.
mobilityBlocks.notes: No silver cord; no physical body remains behind.

combatMechanics.notes: Astral form can affect insubstantial and astral beings. Combat and interaction follow Nightbane RPG astral projection rules (p. 70).

statusAlterations.summary: Becomes a full astral being with no physical body, silver cord, or body left behind.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Subject to astral projection limitations (Nightbane RPG, p. 70). Cannot return to physical form while still on the Astral Plane.

name: Blast Wave
description: Generates a powerful concussive shock wave originating from the Nightbane, expanding outward in all directions or only in front of him. Sends anyone and anything not sufficiently heavy or secured flying several yards away and shatters fragile objects such as windows. Primarily defensive, but can be lethal under the right circumstances (e.g., on a skyscraper roof).

baseActivation.summary: Up to 6 P.P.E. per level of experience (maximum spend per activation)

ppe.notes: Double total P.P.E. spent on activation if used in Facade form.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot selectively affect targets within the radius or forward arc — everyone caught takes damage and is thrown. Exceptions: occupants inside a vehicle, or one to two people in physical contact with the Nightbane who are willed to be protected.

ranges.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius around the Nightbane, or a 40 foot (12.2 m) swath in front; area may be reduced in 3 foot (1 m) increments.

areaOfEffect.summary: 20 foot radius or 40 foot forward swath (adjustable in 3 foot increments).

duration.summary: Instant.

save.summary: Roll with impact 16 or better halves damage; thrown distance, initiative loss, attack loss, and skill penalties are unchanged.
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: Damage halved; all other blast effects unchanged.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius around the Nightbane; area reducible in 3 foot (1 m) increments.
40 foot (12.2 m) wide swath in front of the Nightbane; area reducible in 3 foot (1 m) increments.

rangeAndArea.summary: 20 foot radius or 40 foot forward swath; range/area adjustable in 3 foot (1 m) increments.

rangeMechanics.summary: Originates from the Nightbane; 20 foot radius or 40 foot forward swath, adjustable in 3 foot increments.

damage.summary: 2D6 S.D.C. to each victim caught in the blast

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Full damage plus thrown 1D6 yards, lose initiative, lose two melee attacks, and -5% to all skills for one melee round.

statusEffects.summary: Thrown 1D6 yards (0.9–5.5 m); shatters fragile objects such as windows.

combatMechanics.notes: Non-selective — everyone in the area is affected unless protected (inside a vehicle, or 1–2 people in physical contact with the Nightbane who are willed protected).

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Lose initiative and two melee attacks; -5% to all skills for one melee round. Penalties are not reduced by a successful roll with impact.

statusAlterations.summary: Victims lose initiative, lose two melee attacks, and are shaken (-5% all skills for one melee round).

name: Bomb-Maker
description: Transforms inert substances into lethal explosives that mimic real-world properties (e.g., olive oil into a Molotov cocktail, sawdust into gunpowder, clay into C-4). Blast radius and damage depend on P.P.E. spent and the amount transformed. Created explosives are indistinguishable from genuine substances, detectable and neutralizable by normal means, and are not magical in nature.

baseActivation.summary: 10 P.P.E. per liter/pound of low-grade explosive; 15 P.P.E. per half pound of high-explosive

activationTiers.summary: Low-grade (TNT, gunpowder): 10 P.P.E. per liter or pound — 4D6 S.D.C., 4 ft blast radius
High-explosive (plastique): 15 P.P.E. per half pound — 1D4×10 S.D.C., 3 ft blast radius

ppe.notes: Created explosives mimic real-world properties (nitroglycerin unstable, gunpowder won't ignite when wet, etc.). Substances are not magical and deal no extra damage to beings vulnerable to magic.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only the explosive substance is created — not containers or detonators. C-4 requires an electronic detonator; grenades and land mines require proper casings supplied separately. Material reverts to its original form when the duration expires. Explosives are indistinguishable from genuine substances and can be detected or neutralized by normal means.

ranges.summary: Touch to transmute into an explosive material.

duration.summary: 6 hours per level of experience; then reverts to the original material.

notes: Common among Resistance and Warlords Nightbane.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch to transmute inert material into explosive substance.

damage.summary: 4D6 S.D.C. — low-grade (TNT, gunpowder) per 10 P.P.E.; 1 liter or 1 pound (0.45 kg); 4 foot (1.2 m) blast radius
1D4×10 S.D.C. — high-explosive (plastique) per 15 P.P.E.; half pound (0.225 kg); 3 foot (0.9 m) blast radius

combatMechanics.notes: Not magical — no bonus damage vs magic-vulnerable beings. Blast radius and yield depend on P.P.E. spent and amount transformed.

createdObjects.name: Low-grade explosive
createdObjects.notes: 10 P.P.E. per liter or pound (0.45 kg) of TNT, gunpowder, or similar. 4 foot (1.2 m) blast radius. Mimics real-world instability and handling.

createdObjects.name: High-explosive (plastique)
createdObjects.notes: 15 P.P.E. per half pound (0.225 kg). 3 foot (0.9 m) blast radius. Requires separate detonator/casing for practical use.

damageModifiersNote: Explosives are not magical in nature and do not deal extra damage to beings vulnerable to magic.

name: Bookworm
description: Devours books, magazines, scrolls, or other written sources whole to instantly know every word — regardless of language. Mundane reading grants total recall as if just read; technical material also grants temporary use of 1–4 related skills at 90% (or boosts existing skills to 90%+1D6%). Spell books or scrolls may temporarily allow casting contained spells if the user can already cast magic.

activationTiers.summary: Mundane text: 1 P.P.E. per 25 pages (books, magazines, journals, etc.)
Spell book or scroll: 2 P.P.E. per page (or page equivalent)

ppe.notes: Knowledge or abilities become available one melee round (15 seconds) after the source is devoured.

limitations.otherLimitations: Any source devoured is utterly destroyed. Devoured text ceases to exist after being eaten. Any knowledge gained is not permanent and disappears when the duration lapses. Devouring enchanted or cursed tomes may have unforeseen consequences (G.M. discretion). Cannot cast devoured spells without prior spell-casting ability.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One hour per level of experience.

notes: Diaries and journals may reveal investigation clues. Destroying written knowledge is controversial among Seekers but useful to Nocturne Nightbane in a pinch.

activationTime.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) after devouring before knowledge or abilities are available.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Knows and remembers everything in the source as if just read.
Full recall; may reveal critical investigation clues.
Retains the information and temporarily gains 1–4 related skills at 90% (or boosts existing skills to 90%+1D6%).
Temporarily enables casting contained spell(s) only if the user can already cast magic.

skills.notes: Technical or instructional material only; existing skills may be boosted to 90%+1D6% instead.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Spell-casting from devoured spell books requires prior spell-casting ability. All benefits lapse when the Talent duration ends.

skillsGranted.notes: 1–4 related skills at 90%, or boost existing skills to 90%+1D6%, for the duration of the Talent.

name: Borrow Morphus
description: This rare power allows a Nightbane to assume the Morphus of another of his kind. The copied Nightbane suffers no ill effect other than the shock of seeing his double. The borrower gains the shape, all physical attributes, S.D.C., and Hit Points of the borrowed form, and may use any Talents possessed by the imitated Nightbane that the borrower knew the other possessed. The borrower cannot use his own Talents until he resumes his true form.

enhancement.summary: 15 P.P.E. for each additional minute the borrowed Morphus is kept.

limitations.otherLimitations: Uses the borrower's own combat skills (modified by the borrowed form's attribute bonuses) and normal skills. Does not grant the other person's memories, knowledge, or insanities — only the physical body.

duration.summary: 4 melee rounds (1 minute); 15 P.P.E. per additional minute to extend.

activationTime.summary: Short concentration (a few seconds, equivalent to one melee attack/action), followed by P.P.E. expenditure.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Assumes the target Nightbane's Morphus shape, all physical attributes, S.D.C., and Hit Points. May activate any of the imitated Nightbane's Talents that the borrower knew the other possessed. Uses the borrower's own combat skills (modified by the borrowed form's attribute bonuses) and normal skills — no borrowed memories, knowledge, or insanities.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Cannot use the borrower's own Talents until resuming true form. Transforming/altering Talents generally block other Talents for the duration.

combatMechanics.notes: Combat skills remain the borrower's own, modified by the borrowed Morphus attribute bonuses. The copied Nightbane suffers no ill effect other than the shock of seeing his double.

statusAlterations.summary: Physically becomes another Nightbane's Morphus — borrows body and known Talents only, not mind or memories.

name: Brain Freeze
description: Induces severe, debilitating head pain resembling an extreme “brain freeze,” scrambling the target’s thoughts and imposing heavy combat and skill penalties.

enhancement.summary: If the target failed to save, spend 6 P.P.E. at the start of each subsequent melee round to extend duration by one melee round (up to eight extensions in a row).

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. for the initial attack and 1D4 melee round duration on a failed save. Up to eight additional melee rounds may be purchased at 6 P.P.E. each.

cannotAffect: creatures with physiologies radically different from humans (Nightland Hounds, Hunters, Hollow Men, Tarantuloids, etc.)
limitations.otherLimitations: Affects Night Princes, vampires, and Nightbane in Facade form or very human-looking Morphus forms. Victim cannot remember what they were about to say or do unless obvious; if mid-skill, cannot remember the next step until the freeze ends.

ranges.summary: 200 feet (61 m) + 20 feet (6 m) per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: 1D4 melee rounds (15–60 seconds); extendable up to eight additional melee rounds.

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or better (including P.E. bonus). Failure imposes penalties and blocks spell casting, psionics, and Talent activation until duration ends.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: -5 Perception, -2 all combat rolls, initiative last, -30% all skills, and cannot cast spells, use psionics, or activate Talents until duration ends.

rangeMechanics.summary: 200 feet (61 m) + 20 feet (6 m) per level; line of sight required.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: -5 Perception, -2 all combat rolls, initiative last, -30% skills, cannot cast spells/use psionics/activate Talents; victim may not remember intended action unless obvious.

statusEffects.summary: Cannot cast spells, use psionics, or activate Talents for the duration.
If mid-skill, cannot remember the next step until the freeze ends.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Initiative is automatically last. Penalties last for the duration (1D4 melee rounds base; extendable).

statusAlterations.summary: Severe head pain from between the eyes to the back of the skull; scrambles thoughts and blocks concentration abilities.

name: Bypass
description: Disables conventional and high-tech locks, alarms, and tied surveillance (cameras, sensors) for padlocks, magnetic seals, deadbolts, handcuffs, car doors, safes, and high-security vault systems. Does not affect magical locks or protections.

activationTiers.summary: Simple lock (diary, cheap jewelry box, file cabinet, bedroom/bathroom door): 1 P.P.E. — instant, one melee action
Standard lock (most houses, padlocks, handcuffs, security doors, car doors/trunks/ignitions): 2 P.P.E. — 1D4 melee rounds
Complex lock (prison cells, hospital wards, commercial security doors, common safes, basic alarms/video): 6 P.P.E. — 1D4 minutes; feed goes dark without tripping alarm
Advanced lock (bank vaults, top-secret data centers, military facilities): 14 P.P.E. — 4D4 minutes

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one lock (and its tied security systems) per activation. Disables locking mechanisms and linked alarms/cameras on that door. Complex tier shuts off alarms and video without tripping them — monitors show feed went dark. Does not work on magic locks or magical protections.

ranges.summary: Touch.

duration.summary: Varies by lock tier (instant to 4D4 minutes).

save.summary: Simple lock: roll D20 higher than 2.
Standard lock: roll D20 higher than 5.
Complex lock: roll D20 higher than 8.
Advanced or alien lock: roll D20 higher than 15.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch the lock or door to bypass.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 1 P.P.E.; instant (one melee action). Diary, jewelry box, file cabinet, bedroom/bathroom door, etc. D20 must exceed 2.
2 P.P.E.; 1D4 melee rounds. Most houses, padlocks, handcuffs, security doors, car doors/trunks/ignitions. D20 must exceed 5.
6 P.P.E.; 1D4 minutes. Prison cells, hospital wards, commercial security doors, common safes, basic alarms/video. Feed goes dark without tripping alarm. D20 must exceed 8.
14 P.P.E.; 4D4 minutes. Bank vaults, top-secret data centers, military facilities, high-tech safes. D20 must exceed 15.

activationTime.summary: Instant for simple locks; 1D4 melee rounds (standard), 1D4 minutes (complex), or 4D4 minutes (advanced).
activationTime.notes: Simple tier counts as one melee action.

objectsAffected.summary: One lock and any alarms, cameras, or sensors tied into that lock/door per activation.

savingThrow.notes: Lock difficulty: simple 2, standard 5, complex 8, advanced/alien 15. Failed roll — still locked.

name: Chain Lightning
description: Unleashes a devastating electrical bolt that strikes multiple living targets within a 30 foot radius, jumping about one second apart. Each hop inflicts one die less damage than the prior target. Especially effective against Hounds, Hound Masters, Great Hounds, Hunters, and Darksteel users.

enhancement.summary: Increase initial-target damage by +1D6 per step (up to +7D6 / 10D6 total on the first target). Each hop reduces damage by 1D6.

ppe.notes: 9 P.P.E. base = 3D6 to first target, 2D6 to second, 1D6 to third (up to 10 targets at maximum spend). Maximum total activation spend is 9 P.P.E. per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: Each individual can be struck only once per attack; leftover damage dissipates. Dictating target order beyond the first costs two melee attacks; otherwise hops follow proximity (next closest). Hounds, Hound Masters, Great Hounds, Hunters, and Darksteel users save vs 12+ (P.E. included) or become dazed (−initiative, −2 attacks; cumulative). Hunters and other flying creatures take damage on d8 instead of d6.

ranges.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) + 20 feet (6.1 m) per level to targets within a 30 foot (9.1 m) radius between victims (double range and radius in the Nightlands).

areaOfEffect.summary: 30 foot radius between chain targets (60 foot in the Nightlands).

duration.summary: Instant.

notes: Typical attacks start around 6D6 on the first target rather than maximum output.

rangeMechanics.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) + 20 feet (6.1 m) per level; victims must be within 30 feet (9.1 m) of each other (60 feet in the Nightlands).

rangeAndArea.summary: Chain hops among living targets within 30 foot (9.1 m) of one another; double range and inter-target radius in the Nightlands.

attacksAndDamage.summary: Lightning strikes the first target for full damage, then hops −1D6 per additional target (minimum 1D6). Up to 10 targets at maximum P.P.E. spend; unused damage dissipates.
attacksAndDamage.targetSelectionCost: Dictating hop order beyond the first target costs two melee attacks; otherwise order is by proximity.

damage.summary: 9 P.P.E.: 3D6 → 2D6 → 1D6 across up to three targets
Maximum spend: 10D6 → 9D6 → 8D6 → … → 1D6 across up to ten targets (+3 P.P.E. per +1D6 on first target, up to seven steps)

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Dazed: lose initiative and two attacks for that melee (cumulative with successive attacks).

combatMechanics.notes: Only damages living beings. Hops about one second apart.

damageModifiersNote: Hunters and other flying creatures take damage using eight-sided dice instead of six-sided.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Dazed Hounds/Hunters/Darksteel users lose initiative and two attacks for that melee; penalties are cumulative with successive Chain Lightning attacks.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Save vs 12+ (including P.E.) or become dazed. Hunters and flying creatures take chain damage on d8 instead of d6.

name: Channel Speed
description: One of a handful of Talents meant for use only in Facade form. Taps into the power of the supernatural Morphus form to temporarily add 6 points to P.P. and 10 points to Spd, plus +1 to initiative and +2 to strike, parry, and dodge (in addition to any bonuses from improved P.P.). While active, the user's eyes dim and wisps of shadow rise like thin smoke from the hands and feet. Even in Facade form, the Nightbane registers as a supernatural being to any beings able to sense such things.

ppe.notes: Eight P.P.E. per two melee rounds (30 seconds) of effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only be used in Facade form. Only the bonuses listed in the talent text are added — do not add bonuses from Morphus features.

ranges.summary: Self only.

duration.summary: Two melee rounds (30 seconds) per activation.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self only.

physicalBonusesPenalties.notes: Only the listed Talent bonuses apply — do not add bonuses from Morphus features.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +2 strike, parry, and dodge are in addition to any bonuses from improved P.P.

statusAlterations.summary: Eyes dim; wisps of shadow rise like thin smoke from the hands and feet. Even in Facade form, the Nightbane registers as a supernatural being to senses that can detect such things.

name: Channel Strength
description: One of a handful of Talents meant for use only in Facade form. Temporarily adds 10 points to P.S. and 6 to P.E., plus 60 temporary S.D.C. (damage is subtracted from this S.D.C. first). The character also gains +2 to save vs poison and disease and +2 to save vs psionics. The Facade's strength is converted to Supernatural for the duration. While active, the user's eyes dim and wisps of shadow rise like steam from the body, arms, and legs. Even in Facade form, the Nightbane registers as a supernatural being to any beings able to sense such things.

ppe.notes: Nine P.P.E. per two melee rounds (30 seconds) of effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only be used in Facade form. Only the bonuses listed in the talent text are added — do not add bonuses from Morphus features.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Two melee rounds (30 seconds) per activation.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self.

physicalBonusesPenalties.notes: Facade P.S. is converted to Supernatural for the duration. Only the listed Talent bonuses apply — do not add bonuses from Morphus features.

temporaryResourcePools.summary: 60 temporary S.D.C.; incoming damage is subtracted from this pool first.

rules: Damage is subtracted from the 60 temporary S.D.C. granted by this Talent before normal S.D.C. or Hit Points.

statusAlterations.summary: Eyes dim; wisps of shadow rise like steam from the body, arms, and legs. Even in Facade form, the Nightbane registers as a supernatural being to senses that can detect such things.

name: Chronosphere
description: Alters the temporal flow in the Nightbane's immediate vicinity. Anyone of the user's choosing within the affected radius must save or move at half normal speed, with half the number of melee attacks (rounded up) and a -2 penalty to all combat rolls against anyone unaffected. Anyone entering the area after activation must save as well. Non-magical projectiles and attacks leveled against those within the field are -4 to strike and do half damage as they slow upon entering the area. Can be used in Facade form without any visual manifestation other than the slowed movement of those affected. The talent user is not affected; those who can sense magic or anomalies in space and time will detect the Chronosphere.

ppe.notes: Fourteen P.P.E. per melee round in Morphus (21 P.P.E. per melee round in Facade). Cannot be extended, but may be reactivated after the duration expires or it is canceled.

activation.notes: 14 P.P.E. per melee round in Morphus; 21 P.P.E. per melee round in Facade. In Facade, no visual manifestation except slowed movement of affected targets.

limitations.otherLimitations: User chooses who within the radius is affected. The talent user is not slowed. Anyone entering the area after activation must save or suffer the slowing effects. Detectable by beings that sense magic or temporal anomalies.

ranges.summary: Five foot (1.5 m) spherical radius per level of experience; double the radius for those with a Morphus feature from the Athlete Table.

areaOfEffect.summary: Five foot (1.5 m) spherical radius per level (doubled for Athlete Table Morphus features).

duration.summary: One melee round per activation.

save.summary: Save vs magic 16 or better (including P.E. bonus). Failure: half speed, half melee attacks (rounded up), and -2 to all combat rolls against anyone unaffected by Chronosphere.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Half speed; half melee attacks (round up); -2 to all combat rolls against opponents outside the field.

rangeMechanics.summary: 5 foot (1.5 m) spherical radius per level; 10 foot (3 m) per level with an Athlete table Morphus feature.

rangeAndArea.summary: 5 foot spherical radius per level of experience; radius doubled for Athlete table Morphus features.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Half normal speed; half melee attacks (round up); -2 to all combat rolls against anyone unaffected by the Chronosphere.

statusAlterations.summary: Temporarily slowed temporal flow — half speed and half attacks (round up) on failed save.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Half speed, half attacks (round up), -2 combat vs unaffected opponents.

combatMechanics.notes: Non-magical projectiles and attacks against occupants are -4 to strike and deal half damage as they slow entering the field.

damageModifiersNote: Non-magical attacks leveled against those within the Chronosphere are -4 to strike and do half damage.

name: Dark Edge
description: Many Nightbane refuse to wield liberated Darkblades lest they be marked as Ba'al sympathizers. Dark Edge temporarily imbues any mundane, non-magical blade with the same abilities as a Darkblade, turning the edge ebony. While active, the blade can parry Darkblades without fear of damage, ignores an opponent's A.R., and inflicts double the weapon's normal damage (e.g. a combat knife that normally does 1D6 would inflict 2D6). An imbued blade may be handed off to a friend or ally to use in combat.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per use.

cannotAffect: weapons that are part of a Morphus
limitations.otherLimitations: Non-magical blade weapons only. Imbued blade may be handed to an ally.

ranges.summary: Touch.

duration.summary: Four minutes per level of the talent user.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch a mundane blade weapon to imbue.

createdObjects.name: Dark Edge Imbued Blade
createdObjects.damageRules: Inflicts double the weapon's normal damage; ignores opponent A.R.; may parry Darkblades without damage to the blade.
createdObjects.notes: Blade edge turns ebony and functions like a Darkblade for the duration. May be wielded by the user or an ally.

damage.summary: Double the weapon's normal damage (e.g. 1D6 combat knife → 2D6)

combatMechanics.notes: Ignores opponent A.R. Parry Darkblades without fear of damage to the imbued blade.

damageModifiersNote: Damage dice are doubled from the mundane weapon's normal output.

statusAlterations.summary: Imbued blade edge turns ebony for the duration.

name: Darkblast
description: An improved Shadow Blast. Unlike Shadow Blast, which is almost instantaneous, Darkblast causes shadows to gather around two arms as the attack charges. Once fully charged, the limbs are brought together to fire a large stream of dark energy that packs even more punch than a Shadow Blast. Alternatively, the blast may be fired from the eyes or mouth (or approximate area thereof). Users are +2 to strike with this attack.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per 2D4 damage dealt, up to a maximum of 16 P.P.E. for a 1D6×10 damage blast.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. minimum (2D4 damage). Maximum total activation spend is 3 P.P.E. per level of experience (up to 16 P.P.E. for 1D6×10 damage).

limitations.otherLimitations: Must already know Shadow Blast. Maximum 3 P.P.E. spent on this Talent per level of experience per activation.

duration.summary: Instant once fired; charging takes two melee attacks.

activationTime.summary: Shadows gather while charging; each Darkblast counts as two melee attacks.

rangeMechanics.summary: Fires a large stream up to 1,500 feet (457.2 m). May be discharged from two arms or from the eyes or mouth.

attacksAndDamage.summary: 2 P.P.E. per 2D4 damage; up to 16 P.P.E. for 1D6×10. Total spend capped at 3 P.P.E. per level.

damage.summary: 2 P.P.E.: 2D4 damage (minimum blast)
16 P.P.E. maximum: 1D6×10 damage (subject to 3 P.P.E. per level total cap)

combatMechanics.notes: Improved Shadow Blast. Shadows gather on two arms while charging, then limbs are brought together to fire; alternatively may fire from eyes or mouth.

name: Darkmetal Infusion
description: Reinforces a single stationary object (or portion thereof) with the strength of Nightlands Darkmetal. The targeted object blackens and takes on a dull sheen like Darkmetal ore. In addition to strengthening the object against attack, nothing can pierce the barrier and damage anything behind it — even high-power firearms and Darkblades cannot punch through the object's A.R. Only by depleting the object's total S.D.C. can it be breached.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per 100 S.D.C. added to the reinforced object (maximum 500 S.D.C.).

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. per 100 S.D.C. added. Total activation spend capped at 8 P.P.E. per level of experience (maximum 500 S.D.C. added).

limitations.otherLimitations: Stationary object or portion thereof only. The object cannot be moved or the Talent's effects are immediately negated. Visual effect: object blackens with a dull Darkmetal sheen.

ranges.summary: A 3 foot by 3 foot (1 x 1 m) area per level of experience, up to 3 feet (1 m) away.

areaOfEffect.summary: 3 foot by 3 foot (1 x 1 m) section per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: Up to 3 feet (1 m) away; 3 foot × 3 foot (1 m × 1 m) area per level.

rangeAndArea.summary: 3 foot × 3 foot (1 m × 1 m) reinforced section per level, targeted up to 3 feet (1 m) away.

structureReinforcements.targetSummary: A stationary object or portion thereof.

repeatableScaling.summary: 5 P.P.E. per 100 S.D.C. added, maximum 500 S.D.C.

structureReinforcements.notes: Reinforced object cannot be moved. Nothing pierces the object's A.R. to harm targets behind it — even high-power firearms and Darkblades. Breach only by depleting total S.D.C.

statusAlterations.summary: Target blackens and takes on a dull sheen like Darkmetal ore.

damageModifiersNote: Attacks cannot punch through the reinforced object's A.R.; only total S.D.C. depletion breaches the barrier.

name: Darksong
description: The Nightbane can emit a piercing, deafening sound; it can be an inhuman howl, a mighty roar, an ultrasonic keening, or even electronic feedback, depending on the Nightbane's Morphus. Targets must save vs 10 (P.E. bonuses apply); failure stuns for 1D4 melee rounds.

enhancement.summary: The attacker can raise the number needed to save by expending additional P.P.E.: +1 point per 3 P.P.E.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Stunned for 1D4 melee rounds on a failed save.

save.summary: Save vs 10 (P.E. bonus only). Failure stuns for 1D4 melee rounds. Area use: everyone in the blast, friend or foe, saves at +4 to resist.

notes: Sound manifestation (howl, roar, ultrasonic keening, electronic feedback, etc.) is cosmetic and follows the Nightbane's Morphus.

activationTime.summary: Short concentration (a few seconds, equivalent to one melee attack/action), followed by P.P.E. expenditure.

powerModes.effectsDescription: One target must save vs 10 (P.E. bonus only); failure stuns for 1D4 melee rounds.
60 feet (18.3 m) radius — everyone in the area, friend or foe, must save at +4 to resist.

rangeAndArea.summary: Area mode: 60 foot (18.3 m) radius affecting all occupants, friend or foe.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Stunned for 1D4 melee rounds.
savingThrow.notes: Area use: all in 60 foot radius save at +4. Attacker may raise the save target by +1 per 3 additional P.P.E. spent.

statusAlterations.summary: Piercing, deafening sound — howl, roar, ultrasonic keening, or electronic feedback per Morphus (cosmetic only).

name: Darkwave
description: Becomes an engulfing, semi-liquid mass of pure darkness that can engulf and suffocate opponents. An engulf is a normal melee attack; intended victims dodge at −4. Trapped victims are helpless in absolute darkness, or suffer 2D6 S.D.C. per melee round (not regenerable until freed) while losing half attacks/actions, all combat bonuses, and the use of spells and psionics.

enhancement.summary: 6 P.P.E. per 30 seconds (two melee rounds) maintained.

ppe.notes: 6 P.P.E. per 30 seconds (two melee rounds) after activation.

limitations.otherLimitations: Vulnerable to light; not advisable in daylight — daytime use inflicts 1D6×10 damage at the end of every melee round. Engulf capacity: 1 victim (levels 1–2), 2 (level 3), 3 (level 6), +1 at levels 9, 12, and 15. Multiple victims dodge against one attack roll without penalty.

ranges.summary: Melee engulf while in darkwave form.

duration.summary: 6 P.P.E. per 30 seconds (two melee rounds) maintained.

rangeMechanics.summary: Melee engulf — semi-liquid darkness form engulfs opponents within reach.

activationRequirements.summary: Morphus only. Vulnerable to light; not advisable in daylight.

attacksAndDamage.summary: Engulf counts as a normal melee attack. Optional 2D6 S.D.C. per melee round to trapped victims (not regenerable until freed). Engulf capacity by level: 1 (levels 1–2), 2 (level 3), 3 (level 6), +1 at levels 9, 12, and 15.

holdsAndEscapes.summary: Engulfed victims are helplessly confined in absolute darkness. Multiple victims each dodge against the Nightbane's single attack roll without penalty.
holdsAndEscapes.holdDurationSummary: While darkwave form is maintained and the victim remains engulfed.

rules: Takes half damage from physical attacks directed at the darkness.
Takes double damage from energy and magical attacks from those not trapped inside.

Strong light inflicts damage: no damage from streetlights, 1D6 from a pen light (range: 5 feet/1.5 m), 3D6 from a commercial flashlight (range 10 feet/3 m), 5D6 from a high-powered or police flashlight, and 1D6×10 from a spotlight or magic light or Guardian. (Once per melee round per light wielder).
Cannot be used during the day, or the Nightbane will take 1D6×10 points of damage at the end of every melee round.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Lose half attacks/actions per melee round and all combat bonuses. Spells and psionics are impossible while engulfed.

damage.summary: Optional 2D6 S.D.C. per melee round to engulfed victims at the Nightbane's option.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Helplessly confined in absolute darkness.
savingThrow.notes: Engulf counts as a normal melee attack. Multiple victims dodge against one attack roll without penalty.

statusEffects.summary: Engulfed victims helpless in absolute darkness; optional 2D6 S.D.C. per melee round (not regenerable until freed).

combatMechanics.notes: Nightbane may confine without damage or inflict 2D6 S.D.C. per melee round while victims remain trapped.

statusEffects.name: Engulfed in Darkwave
statusEffects.summary: Helpless in absolute darkness; lose half attacks/actions and all combat bonuses; cannot use spells or psionics.

statusAlterations.summary: Becomes a semi-liquid mass of pure darkness.

damageModifiersNote: Engulfed damage cannot be regenerated until the victim is free of the darkness.
notes: Engulfed victims confined without damage, or confined with 2D6 S.D.C. per melee round at the Nightbane's option.

name: Darkwhip
description: Creates tendrils of darkness that inflict supernatural damage with a P.S. equal to the amount of P.P.E. spent on creating them. The tendrils can have any shape, not limited to whips and tentacles, although those are the most common. The Darkwhip can be used to strike and also to entangle or disarm, provided those skills are known to the wielder.

enhancement.summary: Additional P.P.E. increases the P.S. of the whip, which increases the amount of damage it inflicts.

duration.summary: 4 melee rounds (one minute); must be recreated when it expires.

ppeScaling.summary: Supernatural damage uses a P.S. equal to the total P.P.E. spent on this activation (base plus any additional P.P.E.).

notes: Tendril shape is cosmetic (whips, tentacles, barbed wire, etc.) and follows the Nightbane's Morphus. Entangle and disarm require the wielder to know those skills.

activationTime.summary: Short concentration (a few seconds, equivalent to one melee attack/action), followed by P.P.E. expenditure.

attacksAndDamage.summary: Supernatural damage with P.S. equal to total P.P.E. spent on this activation (base plus any additional P.P.E.). Additional P.P.E. increases damage per strike.

combatMechanics.notes: Strike for supernatural damage; entangle or disarm if the wielder knows those skills. Lasts 4 melee rounds (one minute), then must be recreated.

statusAlterations.summary: Tendrils of darkness — shape varies by Morphus (whip, tentacle, barbed wire, crackling energy, etc.) without changing mechanics.

name: Death-Grip
description: Despite the name, this Talent inflicts no damage. It allows the user to hold tight to objects or opponents with an unbreakable grip stronger than any vise. When subduing an opponent, breaking free requires total Supernatural Strength twice that of the talent user; even then, the target and rescuers must beat the Nightbane's P.S. x2. The Nightbane cannot be disarmed of handheld weapons, nor can an object be pulled or pried from his grip. Useful for clinging to moving vehicles, aircraft, flying monsters, or objects to avoid falling. Adds +20% to Climbing and +3 to entangle for the duration.

enhancement.summary: Six P.P.E. per additional melee round the grip is maintained.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per melee round of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Inflicts no damage — even on fragile objects or a victim's throat. Useful for clinging to moving vehicles, aircraft, flying monsters, or objects to avoid falling.

ranges.summary: Touch/grasp.

duration.summary: One melee round per activation (extendable at 6 P.P.E. per melee round).

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch/grasp an object or opponent.

holdsAndEscapes.summary: Unbreakable grip stronger than any vise. Breaking free requires total Supernatural P.S. at least twice the talent user's; rescuers must beat the Nightbane's P.S. ×2.
holdsAndEscapes.breakFreeRequirement: Supernatural P.S. twice the talent user's

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Cannot be disarmed of handheld weapons; gripped objects cannot be pulled or pried free.

combatMechanics.notes: Delivers no damage. Cannot be disarmed; objects and opponents held cannot be pulled free without meeting the escape requirements.

statusAlterations.summary: Unbreakable hold for subduing opponents, climbing, or clinging to vehicles or flying creatures.

name: Dervish Dance
description: Spins in a blurring circle within a 10 foot (3 m) diameter dance zone. Opponents inside or entering are struck as if the Nightbane rolled 10 + 1 per level to hit (maximum 19). Retaliation by anyone in the zone triggers another strike pass. Uses the same number to auto-parry incoming physical missiles (not energy). Lasts one full melee round, must be the first attack that round, and uses all melee attacks for the round.

enhancement.summary: 20 P.P.E. per additional melee round of Dervish Dance.

ppe.notes: 20 P.P.E. per melee round the dance is maintained.

limitations.otherLimitations: 10 foot (3 m) diameter dance zone. Must be the first attack of the melee round and counts as all melee attacks that round. Opponents may dodge normally, parry at −6, or roll with punch/fall/impact.

ranges.summary: 10 foot (3 m) diameter dance zone centered on the Nightbane.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds); additional melee rounds cost 20 P.P.E. each.

rangeMechanics.summary: 10 foot (3 m) diameter dance zone (5 foot radius) centered on the Nightbane.

activationRequirements.summary: Must be the Nightbane's first attack of the melee round. Uses all melee attacks for that round.

attacksAndDamage.summary: Strike as if attack roll 10 + 1 per level (maximum 19); damage as individual punches. Re-strike all zone occupants when any occupant attacks the dancer.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Auto-parry incoming physical missiles (bullets, arrows, etc.) at the same strike roll (10 + level, maximum 19). Energy attacks strike and damage as normal. Outsiders striking in must beat the parry roll + 1 to hit.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Opponents in the dance zone may dodge normally, parry at −6, or roll with punch/fall/impact after being struck.

damage.summary: Damage as individual punches per Morphus supernatural P.S.

savingThrow.notes: Fixed strike roll 10 + level (maximum 19) — no attack die roll required. Saver wins ties on dodge. Occupants who leave the diameter are safe from further dance strikes.

combatMechanics.notes: All opponents within or entering the zone are struck on activation. Re-attack everyone in the zone when any occupant retaliates against the dancer.

statusAlterations.summary: Spins at blurring speed in a circular dance pattern within the zone.

notes: Re-attack everyone in the zone when any occupant attacks the dancer. Outsiders striking in must beat parry roll + 1 to hit.

name: Doorway
description: Creates a door of dark energy that leads to a specific location desired by the creator of it. The Nightbane must have been to the destination point at least once in his life, or he must have line of sight.

activationTiers.name: Nightlands Doorway
activationTiers.effectDescription: Opens a doorway to the Nightlands or back.

rangeOverride.summary: 40 P.P.E. plus 5 P.P.E. per mile (1.6 km) to cross over.

enhancement.summary: 2 P.P.E. spent per mile of distance.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must have visited the destination at least once, or have line of sight to it.

ranges.summary: To a location up to 1 mile (1.6 km) per 2 P.P.E. spent.

activationTime.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) of concentration

activationRequirements.summary: Destination must have been visited at least once, or be within line of sight.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Prior visit or line of sight to destination

rangeMechanics.summary: Up to 1 mile (1.6 km) per 2 P.P.E. spent to the chosen destination.
rangeMechanics.notes: Nightlands crossing: 40 P.P.E. base plus 5 P.P.E. per mile (1.6 km).

powerModes.effectsDescription: Minimum 2 P.P.E.; 1 mile (1.6 km) range per 2 P.P.E. spent to a previously visited location or one in line of sight.
40 P.P.E. to open a doorway to the Nightlands or back, plus 5 P.P.E. per mile (1.6 km).

environmentalConstructs.summary: Door of dark energy linking the caster's position to the chosen destination.

statusAlterations.summary: Opens a traversable portal of dark energy to a specific desired location.

notes: Minimum 2 P.P.E. to activate for a normal doorway. Nightlands crossings use the separate tier (40 P.P.E. base + 5 P.P.E. per mile).

name: Down the Drain
description: Primarily an urban escape Talent. Liquefies the user and up to two passengers (plus personal clothing/gear) to travel through plumbing, pipes, or sewer systems. The Nightbane controls movement as liquid — including against water pressure, tide, waves, and gravity. If the duration ends before reaching a place that can accommodate returning to humanoid form, the traveler must automatically reactivate at the usual cost. Movement speed is a constant 1,000 feet (305 m) per minute unless the current is faster. In liquid form, travelers are immune to toxins/poisons and all physical attacks but cannot attack, even with psionics. Filtration systems have no detrimental effect, though the experience is disorienting and unpleasant.

enhancement.summary: Six P.P.E. per additional five minutes in liquefied form.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per five minutes liquefied. Must automatically reactivate at usual cost if duration ends before reaching a suitable exit.

limitations.otherLimitations: Other than escaping down a drain, moving through plumbing, or extending/deactivating this Talent, no other actions are possible in liquid form — including psionic attacks. Large, heavy gear (large suitcase, duffel bag, bulky equipment) is left behind. Only the user and up to two others plus personal clothing/gear may transform. Sewage filtration systems cause no harm but the trip is disorienting and unpleasant.

ranges.summary: Self and up to two others by touch. Must be within 3 feet (0.9 m) of a drain or other open plumbing.

duration.summary: Five minutes per activation (extendable at 6 P.P.E. per five minutes).

save.summary: Unwilling travelers save vs magic 12 or better. Success resists transformation and liquid transport.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Transformed and transported in liquid form with the user.

activationRequirements.summary: Must be within 3 feet (0.9 m) of a drain or other open plumbing to liquefy and enter the system.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self and up to two others by touch; must start within 3 feet (0.9 m) of open plumbing.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Movement speed is a constant 1,000 feet (305 m) per minute unless the current is faster. Can move against pressure, tide, waves, and gravity.

statusEffects.name: Liquefied Form
statusEffects.summary: Immune to toxins/poisons and all physical attacks; cannot attack (including psionics). -4 on Perception Rolls.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: All liquefied travelers suffer distorted, disorienting surroundings.

rules: Immune to toxins/poisons and all physical attacks while liquefied.
Sewage filtration systems have no detrimental effect.

combatMechanics.notes: Cannot attack in liquid form — not even with psionics.

statusAlterations.summary: Liquid appearance may resemble motor oil, sludge, sap, or blood. Only travel, extension, or deactivation are permitted.

name: Dreamer
description: Enters the Dreamstream body and soul. While there, psychic S.D.C. equals normal S.D.C. and Hit Points combined. Other Talents work normally; the Nightbane may enter others' Dream Pools. Cannot manipulate dreamstuff to terrorize dreamers, but Morphus shape and presence often cause nightmares.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. to enter or leave the Dreamstream. No ongoing cost once inside.

limitations.otherLimitations: If psychic S.D.C. falls below zero, the character is slain and vanishes as if he never existed. May remain in the Dreamstream indefinitely once entered.

ranges.summary: Self — enters the Dreamstream body and soul.

duration.summary: Indefinite while in the Dreamstream; 10 P.P.E. to enter or leave.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — full entry into the Dreamstream (body and soul).

temporaryResourcePools.summary: Psychic S.D.C. equals combined physical S.D.C. and Hit Points while in the Dreamstream.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Other Talents work normally in the Dreamstream. May enter other people's Dream Pools. Cannot manipulate dreamstuff to terrorize dreamers; Morphus shape and presence often cause nightmares anyway. See Between the Shadows Dreamstream section for full travel and combat rules.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Dreamstream travel — may enter others' Dream Pools per Between the Shadows Dreamstream rules.
mobilityBlocks.notes: Enters body and soul; no separate physical body left behind on Earth.

rules: While in the Dreamstream, damage applies to psychic S.D.C. (equal to combined physical S.D.C. and Hit Points).
If psychic S.D.C. falls below zero, the character is slain and vanishes as if he never existed.

combatMechanics.notes: Cannot manipulate dreamstuff to terrorize dreamers. Other Talents function normally. Morphus appearance alone may spawn nightmares. Full Dreamstream combat and interaction rules are in Between the Shadows.

statusAlterations.summary: Enters the Dreamstream body and soul — present in dream realms rather than the physical world.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: If psychic S.D.C. is reduced below zero, the Nightbane is slain and vanishes as if he never existed.

name: Dust in the Wind
description: Favored by ancient Nightbane who prize self-preservation. If activated before the character reaches zero Hit Points, instead of falling into a coma or perishing, the user disintegrates into millions of black dust particles whisked away on a magical breeze to a predetermined safe point (up to 2,000 feet/610 m away), then reconstitutes. The character falls into a coma in whatever form they were in when reduced to zero Hit Points until regenerating to full Hit Points and at least half S.D.C. If more than 20% of the dust is prevented from reaching the safe point, the Nightbane remains in dust form for 1 hour per M.E. attribute point, then reconstitutes wherever the dust is — minus an arm or leg and -10 Hit Points for every 10% missing (permanent losses).

ppe.notes: Twenty-five P.P.E. to activate. Must designate a safe haven before first use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must be activated before reaching zero Hit Points. A return point or safe haven must be designated before use. While in dust form the Nightbane is unconscious and mindlessly homes in on that haven at 40 mph (64 kph).

ranges.summary: Self; dust travels up to 2,000 feet (610 m) to the designated safe point.

duration.summary: Transformation takes two melee attacks (~5–6 seconds). Remains dust in hiding until full Hit Points and half S.D.C. are regenerated.

activationRequirements.summary: A safe haven/return point must be designated before this Talent can be used.

activationTime.summary: Disintegration into dust takes two melee attacks (~5–6 seconds).

recoveryStateUntilThreshold.notes: Reconstitutes into a coma at the safe haven until regeneration thresholds are met.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Dust cloud travels to the safe haven at 40 mph (64 kph), up to 2,000 feet (610 m).

rules: Immune to physical attacks while in granular dust form.
Magical/psionic/Talent damage that would reduce the dust form below zero Hit Points by more than the character's P.E. attribute kills the Nightbane.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: If more than 20% of dust cannot reach the safe point: remain dust 1 hour per M.E. point, then reconstitute wherever dust remains — lose an arm or leg and -10 Hit Points permanently for every 10% of dust missing.

combatMechanics.notes: Activated before reaching 0 Hit Points to avoid death/coma from that triggering blow. Safe haven examples: car trunk, box truck, under stairs, abandoned building, dumpster, supply closet, parking-structure corner, rooftop, ditch.

statusAlterations.summary: Disintegrates into millions of black dust particles whisked away on a magical breeze, then reconstitutes at the safe haven in a healing coma.

name: Earthworks
description: Shapes the ground beneath the user at will — natural rock or dirt, or manmade asphalt or concrete — to create defensive walls, barriers, cover, trenches, or escape tunnels. Attacks that beat a barrier's Natural A.R. penetrate to the other side but strike as wild/blind shots. Offensive uses include rolling earth to knock enemies down (or force Piloting rolls at -20% for vehicles) and crash barriers placed in front of moving vehicles (initiative contest; Piloting -30% to dodge).

activationTiers.summary: Temporary barrier or earth shaping: 5 P.P.E.
Permanent structure: 32 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: One additional load per activation at levels 7, 11, and 15. Temporary structures last 2D4+2 minutes unless made permanent.

limitations.otherLimitations: Total volume per load: 360 cubic feet (10.2 m³) when shaping earth; doubled if tunneling only (6 ft × 6 ft × 20 ft tunnel). Shaping control lasts one melee round (15 seconds). Non-permanent earth returns to its original shape after 2D4+2 minutes.

ranges.summary: 5 feet (1.5 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Shaping control lasts one melee round. Temporary walls/barriers/trenches/tunnels remain 2D4+2 minutes unless permanent.
duration.notes: Random duration applies to non-permanent structures after shaping ends.

activationTime.summary: Control and shaping of one load lasts one melee round (15 seconds).

rangeMechanics.summary: 5 feet + 5 feet per level. Total volume 360 cubic feet per load (720 if tunneling only).

powerModes.effectsDescription: Create walls, cover, trenches, or escape tunnels from dirt, clay, asphalt, or concrete (see material stats).
Ground rises and rolls under enemies — knockdown, lose initiative and one attack. Vehicles: Piloting -20% or crash/off-road.
Initiative contest to raise a wall in front of a vehicle; Piloting -30% to dodge/stop/swerve. Failed roll crashes into the barrier.

environmentalConstructs.notes: Light/medium firearms: 1 damage, no penetration. Heavy weapons: 70% damage to barrier, may shoot through.
Small/medium firearms and shotguns: 1 damage, no penetration. Heavy weapons/energy: half damage, good chance to shoot through.
Small/medium firearms: 1 damage, no penetration. Heavy weapons/energy: half damage, poor chance to shoot through.
Most firearms: no structural damage, no penetration. Heavy weapons/AP/explosives/energy: one-third damage, fair chance to shoot through.

savingThrow.notes: Attacks beating a barrier's Natural A.R. penetrate but strike as wild/blind shots. Stone/concrete penetration: half damage through wall, -6 to strike, no Called Shot without line of sight.

statusEffects.summary: Rolling earth: targets lose initiative and one melee attack; drivers Piloting -20%.
Crash barrier: vehicle Piloting -30% to avoid impact if the Nightbane wins initiative.

damageModifiersNote: Penetrating shots through barriers are wild/blind; stone/concrete hits on the far side are at -6 to strike.

name: Eavesdrop
description: Hearing becomes equivalent to a parabolic listening device. The Nightbane can focus on one person or a small group and listen from hundreds of feet away while blocking out all but the loudest background noise. Conversations behind a thin wall or window can be heard as if standing in the room. Does not provide language translation.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per additional minute of listening.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per minute of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Does not translate languages. Phone intercepts may be one-sided unless device audio could be overheard while adjacent to the target (G.M. discretion).

ranges.summary: 200 feet (61 m) + 50 feet (15.2 m) per level of experience; line of sight to target or location required.

duration.summary: One minute per 2 P.P.E. expended.

activationRequirements.summary: At least one target or a specific visible location (street corner, rooftop, window, etc.) must be in line of sight.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Line of sight to target or location

rangeMechanics.summary: 200 feet (61 m) + 50 feet (15.2 m) per level; parabolic focus on one person or small group.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Hearing equivalent to a parabolic listening device — focus on one person or small group, blocking all but the loudest background noise. Conversations through thin walls or windows are heard as if in the room.

statusAlterations.summary: Supernatural directional hearing at extreme range while the Talent is active.

name: Encryption
description: Automatically encrypts any form of communication the Nightbane uses so only intended recipients hear and understand clearly. Written notes appear as symbols; shouted warnings sound like nonsense syllables or a roar to everyone else; surface thoughts are scrambled against Telepathy. Defeated only by magical translation (Tongues or Eyes of Thoth skill) or recording the message and attempting Cryptography at -40%.

enhancement.summary: One P.P.E. per additional minute of encrypted communication.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per minute of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Defeated only by magical translation (Tongues or Eyes of Thoth skill), or by recording the message and attempting Cryptography at -40%.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One minute per P.P.E. expended.

activationRequirements.summary: The Nightbane designates intended recipient(s) who hear and understand clearly; all others perceive scrambled communication.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — affects all communication the user produces while active.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Automatically encrypts speech, writing, and even surface thoughts. Written notes appear as symbols; shouted warnings sound like nonsense syllables or a roar to non-recipients; Telepathy against surface thoughts is scrambled.

statusAlterations.summary: Only designated recipients hear or read intelligible messages; everyone else perceives encrypted symbols, nonsense syllables, or unintelligible roar.

name: Fear No Evil
description: Temporarily negates fear before entering conflict with supernatural foes — Nightlords, Night Princes, demonic minions, monstrous enemies, or fellow Nightbane. For the duration, the user is immune to Horror Factor and temporarily negates fear caused by Phobias. Useful for newly inducted Nightbane who have not yet hardened to the war in the shadows.

enhancement.summary: Four P.P.E. per additional minute beyond the base two minutes.

ppe.notes: Base activation grants two minutes (8 melee rounds) of effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: If still engaged when Fear No Evil lapses and Horror Factor was not rolled or was failed, the character must immediately save vs Horror Factor. While active, -2 on all Perception Rolls.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Two minutes (8 melee rounds); extendable at 4 P.P.E. per additional minute.
duration.notes: Base activation grants 8 melee rounds of effect.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Immune to Horror Factor and temporarily negates fear caused by Phobias for the duration. Best used before entering conflict with supernatural foes.

statusAlterations.summary: Immune to Horror Factor; Phobia-induced fear is temporarily negated while active.

savingThrow.notes: If still engaged when Fear No Evil lapses and Horror Factor was not rolled or was failed, the character must immediately save vs Horror Factor.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: -2 on all Perception Rolls while Fear No Evil is active.

name: Fire Breather
description: Exhales a flamethrower-like jet inflicting 5D6 damage along its path unless blocked by a wall, vehicle, or large object (including a large Nightbane); otherwise flames burn everything to full range. Impervious to own flames, natural fire, and heat for the melee round used — full damage from magical fire or other Nightbane breath attacks.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. per melee round of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Once per melee round; counts as an extra melee attack.

ranges.summary: 30 feet (9.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) per activation.

rangeMechanics.summary: 30 feet (9.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level — flamethrower-like jet along a straight path.

activationRequirements.summary: Once per melee round; counts as an extra melee attack.

attacksAndDamage.summary: 5D6 damage along the breath path unless blocked by a wall, vehicle, or large object (including a large Nightbane).

rules: Impervious to own flames, natural fire, and heat for the melee round the breath is used.
Takes full damage from magical fire or the breath attacks of other Nightbane.

damage.summary: 5D6 damage to anything caught in the jet's path.

combatMechanics.notes: Flames continue to full range unless stopped by a wall, vehicle, or large object. Once per melee round; counts as an extra melee attack.

statusAlterations.summary: Exhales a powerful flamethrower-like jet of fire.

notes: Fire immunity lasts only for the melee round the breath is used.

name: Flurry
description: Named for the volume of blows it enables. Upon activation, grants preternatural combat speed: +2 attacks per melee round, +2 initiative, +2 to strike, parry, and disarm, +3 to dodge. When directly fighting a Nightlord, Night Prince, Night Princess, Greater Demon, or dark god, gains an additional +1 melee attack and +1 to each of those bonuses.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per melee round Flurry remains in place.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot be used in combination with other Talents that provide bonus attacks (e.g. Fire Breather, Dervish Dance).

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One melee round per 10 P.P.E. spent.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Additional +1 melee attack and +1 to initiative, strike, parry, dodge, and disarm when directly fighting a Nightlord, Night Prince, Night Princess, Greater Demon, or dark god.

statusAlterations.summary: Preternatural combat speed — the Nightbane rains down blows at supernatural tempo while Flurry is active.

combatMechanics.notes: Cannot combine with Talents that grant bonus attacks (Fire Breather, Dervish Dance). Ten P.P.E. per melee round the effect remains in place.

name: Form-Lock
description: Neutralizes shape-changing abilities. When a shape-changing being is targeted and fails a save vs magic, it cannot alter its physical form or have it altered by any other means for two minutes (or longer with additional P.P.E.). Applies regardless of whether the ability is natural, magical, or otherwise — vampires cannot mist-shift, Nightlords cannot alter size or appearance (though altering other matter is unaffected), and Nightbane are trapped in their current Facade or Morphus form. Nightbane known to possess Form-Lock may face suspicion from their brethren.

enhancement.summary: Twenty P.P.E. per additional minute beyond the initial two minutes.

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. to Form-Lock one target for two minutes — P.P.E. is spent whether or not the save succeeds.

limitations.otherLimitations: Nightlord ability to alter other matter is unaffected. Nightbane trapped in Facade are practically defenseless — characters known to possess Form-Lock may face suspicion and fear from other Nightbane.

ranges.summary: Line of sight up to 500 feet (152 m) away.

duration.summary: Two minutes base; extendable at 20 P.P.E. per additional minute.

activationRequirements.summary: One shape-changing target within line of sight up to 500 feet (152 m).

save.summary: Save vs magic (15+). Failure: cannot alter physical form or have it altered by any means for the duration. P.P.E. is spent whether or not the save succeeds.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Cannot alter physical form or have it altered by any other means — vampires cannot mist-shift, Nightlords cannot alter size/appearance, Nightbane are trapped in Facade or Morphus.

rangeMechanics.summary: Line of sight up to 500 feet (152 m).

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Cannot alter physical form or have it altered by any means for the duration.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: No form-lock effect; P.P.E. is still spent.
savingThrow.notes: Applies regardless of whether shape-changing is natural, magical, or otherwise.

statusEffects.summary: Vampires cannot change into mist or animal form; Nightlords cannot alter size or appearance; Nightbane are trapped in current Facade or Morphus.

statusAlterations.summary: Affected target cannot alter its physical form or have it altered by any other means until Form-Lock lapses.

name: Frightener
description: For Nightbane who are not naturally terrifying — or who want to be more so. Even those with Awe Factor (such as many Barbies and Kens) see it become Horror Factor for the duration. Manifestation is individual (glowing eyes, black aura, bleeding eyes, deepened shadows, seeming to grow in stature, corpse-like appearance, etc.) but changes are magical illusions created by the Talent. The user cannot select who is affected; allies who know the Nightbane and this Talent are immune unless they must fight or restrain the character.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per +1 bonus to Horror Factor desired, up to a maximum Horror Factor of 15.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per +1 Horror Factor at activation; maximum Horror Factor 15.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot select who is and isn't affected. Allies who know the Nightbane and this Talent are immune to the Horror Factor unless fighting or restraining the character. Maximum Horror Factor while using this Talent is 15. Manifestation changes are magical illusions only.

ranges.summary: Self; added fear affects others who see the character up to 90 feet (27.4 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level.

areaOfEffect.summary: 90 feet (27.4 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level — those who see the character.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds).

rangeMechanics.summary: Horror Factor affects viewers within 90 feet (27.4 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level who see the character.

save.summary: Viewers within range must save vs Horror Factor equal to P.P.E. spent ÷ 2 (2 P.P.E. per +1 HF, maximum 15). Allies who know the user and this Talent are immune unless fighting or restraining the character.

statusAlterations.summary: Magical illusion makes the user seem scarier and more intimidating — glowing eyes, black aura, bleeding eyes, deepened shadows, greater stature, corpse-like appearance, etc. Awe Factor becomes Horror Factor for the duration.

savingThrow.notes: Horror Factor = P.P.E. spent ÷ 2 (2 P.P.E. per +1, maximum 15). Applies to all who see the character within range unless ally immunity applies.

statusEffects.summary: Allies who know the Nightbane and Frightener are immune unless they must fight or restrain the character.

name: Ghost Tag
description: Leaves spectral messages (words or images) on surfaces — walls, sidewalks, vehicles, clothing, even people (who must remain still). Visible to Nightbane, Guardians, Astral Travelers, and those who can See the Invisible. Sensitive psychics may read tags with Perception 12+ when looking for them (16+ if unaware). Nightlords, avatars, and Night Princes can also see tags; Hounds and Hunters cannot. Other minions need See the Invisible or a Sensitive psychic actively searching.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per additional minute to write another 30 words beyond the first 30.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. covers up to 30 words or a recognizable image (~30 words per minute at 4–6 inch lettering).

limitations.otherLimitations: G.M. discretion on message size; ~30 words per minute at 4–6 inch (10–15 cm) lettering is a good guideline. People must remain still while being tagged. Removed by creator at no P.P.E. cost, or overwritten by another Ghost Tag user.

ranges.summary: Cast upon a surface up to 6 feet (1.8 m) away by writing in the air with a finger.

duration.summary: Up to one week per level of experience unless removed by creator or overwritten.
duration.notes: Duration unit is weeks per level.

activationRequirements.summary: Write in the air with a finger to cast the message on a surface within 6 feet (1.8 m). People used as a surface must remain still.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Target surface or still subject within range

activationTime.summary: Approximately one minute per 30 words at 4–6 inch (10–15 cm) lettering.

rangeMechanics.summary: Cast upon a surface up to 6 feet (1.8 m) away.

createdObjects.name: Ghost Tag
createdObjects.notes: Spectral words or images on walls, sidewalks, vehicles, clothing, or people. Auto-visible to Nightbane, Guardians, Astral Travelers, and those with See the Invisible. Example tags: "Safe Zone," "Nightbane Underground Railroad," "Portal to the Nightlands," "Traitor," etc.

save.summary: Sensitive psychics (without See the Invisible) when looking for tags or knowing one is present.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Cannot find or read the Ghost Tag.

save.summary: Sensitive psychics unaware of the tag and not actively searching.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Cannot find or read the Ghost Tag.

combatMechanics.notes: Auto-visible to Nightbane, Guardians, Astral Travelers, and See the Invisible (spell or psionic). Nightlords and avatars see tags; Night Princes roll Perception like psychics without See the Invisible. Hounds and Hunters cannot see Ghost Tags. Other minions need See the Invisible or an actively searching Sensitive psychic.

statusAlterations.summary: Leaves a spectral graffiti-style message visible only to eligible supernatural observers — speculated to be a kind of illusion.

name: Gunslinger
description: Turns the user into a savant with modern firearms — revolvers, pistols, rifles, assault weapons, rocket launchers, and any projectile-firing gun. The held weapon becomes an extension of the Morphus form, usable with expert precision regardless of prior training. While active: +1 attack per melee with firearms, +3 initiative when drawing/firing, +2 strike and disarm on Aimed/Called Shots (even on the move), +1 strike with bursts, +2 strike and parry using the gun as a melee weapon, no penalty for shooting while running, from vehicles, or unconventional positions. Can "see" through the weapon's sights/barrel for blindfolded or behind-the-back shots. Morphus/body-integrated firearms gain an additional +1 to strike on ranged attacks. Stacks with W.P. skill bonuses.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per melee round Gunslinger remains in place.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one type of firearm receives Gunslinger bonuses at a time (multiple weapons of that type do not multiply bonuses). Other weapons may be used simultaneously but do not gain these bonuses.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One full melee round (15 seconds) per 10 P.P.E. spent.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — bonuses apply to the held firearm category only.

activationRequirements.summary: Must wield a modern firearm (revolver, pistol, rifle, assault weapon, rocket launcher, or any projectile-firing gun). Only one firearm type receives bonuses at a time.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +1 strike with bursts; +1 strike on ranged attacks when the firearm is part of the Morphus. +2 strike and disarm on Aimed or Called Shots even on the move.

statusAlterations.summary: Held firearm becomes an extension of the Morphus form — savant-level precision regardless of prior training. User can see through the weapon's sights or barrel even when blindfolded or shooting behind the back (weapon must point that direction).

combatMechanics.notes: No penalty for shooting while running, leaping, from a speeding vehicle, or unconventional positions (hanging upside down, swinging from a rope, etc.). Stacks with W.P. skill bonuses. Other weapons may be used simultaneously but do not gain Gunslinger bonuses. Ten P.P.E. per melee round.

name: Heal Facade
