description: Extends Morphus superhuman regeneration to the Facade for a limited time. While active, the Facade heals 1D6+4 S.D.C./Hit Points per melee round.

enhancement.summary: 5 P.P.E. for each subsequent melee round of healing.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. for the first melee round; 5 P.P.E. per each additional melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Healing applies only to the Facade.

ranges.summary: Self (Facade form).

duration.summary: One melee round per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — Facade form only.

healing.summary: Facade heals 1D6+4 S.D.C./Hit Points per melee round while active.

combatMechanics.notes: Healing applies only to the Facade. Base activation covers the first melee round; 5 P.P.E. per each subsequent melee round. Duration cap: one melee round per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Extends Morphus superhuman regeneration to the Facade for the duration.

name: Hotwire
description: Similar to the elite Deus ex Machina Talent. While seated behind the wheel, temporarily takes control of any motorized vehicle: unlock doors/trunk, start/stop without keys, deactivate alarms, operate minor automated systems (door locks, wipers), and pilot even without prior training at 66% + 2% per level. Stunt driving at half normal penalties; +15% to existing piloting skill for the vehicle type while active. Popular with Warlords for car theft; useful to Resistance and Nocturnes for hijacking aircraft, boats, and military vehicles.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per activation grants fifteen minutes of control per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: Motorized vehicles under their own power only; controls must be physically operated (minor electronics manipulated by will). While piloting, the user can only speak — contact broken or forced into combat ends control (one melee action to re-link if Talent still active). Another Hotwire user can usurp control (D20 + M.E. bonus, high roll wins, defender wins ties); Deus ex Machina always wins. Single-pilot vehicles only — on crewed vehicles, only pilot/helmsman controls (not co-pilot, gunner, navigator, sensor operator, communications, engineer, etc.).

ranges.summary: Touch (must be behind the wheel).

duration.summary: Fifteen minutes per level of experience.

activationRequirements.summary: Must sit behind the wheel of a motorized vehicle that travels under its own power.

activationTime.summary: One melee action to re-establish vehicle control after contact is broken, while Hotwire remains active.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch — user must be at the vehicle controls.

grantedSkills.notes: Base 66% + 2% per level when the character lacks the skill; +15% if already trained in that vehicle type. Stunt driving and trick maneuvers at half normal penalties.

savingThrow.notes: Hotwire vs Hotwire usurp contest: D20 + M.E. bonus each; high roll wins, defender wins ties. Deus ex Machina always succeeds in usurping Hotwire.

combatMechanics.notes: While piloting, the user can only speak. Losing contact or entering hand-to-hand combat ends control until re-linked.

statusAlterations.summary: Unlocks doors/trunk, starts/stops without keys, deactivates car alarms, and operates minor automated systems (electronic locks, wipers, etc.) through force of will.

name: Hound Leash
description: Temporarily takes control of Hounds, Hunters, and Great Hounds (not Hound Masters), commanding them like trained guard dogs — even against other minions. Starts with one target; an additional Hound may be controlled at levels 6, 9, 12, and 15. If not all targets succumb, the Talent may be used again on remaining targets up to the maximum without releasing those already leashed. Leashed creatures can attack their own kind but cannot be ordered to destroy themselves or attack a Hound Master — doing so instantly breaks the Leash and may turn them on the controller.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per each leashed Hound or Hunter to extend duration one additional minute (no new save).

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per Hound or Hunter leashed (or attempted — spent whether or not the save succeeds).

limitations.otherLimitations: Must have previously encountered a Hound, Hunter, or similar before selecting this Talent. Controls one Hound/Hunter/Great Hound at a time; an additional target at levels 6, 9, 12, and 15 (maximum five at level 15+). If not all targets succumb, may use Hound Leash again on remaining targets up to the maximum without releasing those already leashed. Each controlled Hound reduces the user's attacks per melee by one. Unconsciousness or moving beyond range breaks the Leash. Hound Masters are impervious and become angry when control over their minions is stolen.

ranges.summary: 10 feet (3 m) + 2 feet (0.6 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute; extendable at 5 P.P.E. per leashed target per additional minute (no new save).

activationRequirements.summary: Target Hound, Hunter, or Great Hound within range. Ten P.P.E. spent per target attempted (whether or not the save succeeds).

save.summary: Target Hound or Hunter saves vs magic (15+). Failure: temporarily enslaved to verbal commands like trained guard dogs.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Leashed minion obeys verbal commands and may protect the user even against other minions.
save.effectsOnSuccess: P.P.E. still spent; target remains free.

rangeMechanics.summary: 10 feet (3 m) + 2 feet (0.6 m) per level.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Temporarily leashed to verbal commands; may attack other minions including its own kind.
savingThrow.notes: Hound Masters are impervious. Ordering self-destruction or an attack on a Hound Master instantly breaks the Leash and may turn the minion on the controller.

statusEffects.summary: Leashed Hounds/Hunters/Great Hounds follow verbal commands but cannot be ordered to destroy themselves or attack a Hound Master.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Each Hound or Hunter controlled reduces the user's attacks per melee round by one. Leash breaks if the user is knocked unconscious or moves beyond range.

statusAlterations.summary: Temporarily steals control of Nightlands hunting demons, commanding them like trained guard dogs.

name: Hyper-Awareness
description: Greatly increases awareness and observational capacity. Upon activation, the character's eyes glow faintly and ears enlarge slightly. Provides +3 to all Perception Rolls, +1 to dodge, and +10% to awareness skills such as Detect Ambush, Detect Concealment, Seduction, Surveillance Systems/Tailing, Tracking, and similar.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per additional minute of heightened awareness.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. to activate; five P.P.E. for each additional minute, as desired.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One minute per 5 P.P.E. spent.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self.

physicalBonusesPenalties.notes: +10% to skills that require awareness: Detect Ambush, Detect Concealment, Seduction, Surveillance Systems/Tailing, Tracking, and similar.

statusAlterations.summary: Eyes glow faintly and ears enlarge slightly while observational capacity is heightened.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: +10% to awareness-based skills for the duration.

name: Ignorance is Bliss
description: Tricks mortal witnesses into seeing and remembering what they want to believe rather than supernatural truth — useful when loved ones or bystanders witness transformations or monster battles. Conceals nothing from supernatural beings, psychics, magic users, or those with solid belief or firsthand paranormal experience. Skeptics who want the truth may save vs magic (15+); disbelievers auto-succumb with permanent false memories. Can cover recent events retroactively or run preemptively when trouble is expected.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. lets everyone susceptible in the radius believe and remember what they want to believe.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only disguises the supernatural from those who do not believe or do not want to believe it exists (most Preeverts, unthrall local police/firemen, and the public at large). Effects are permanent false memories for those who succumb. Witness reports from affected mortals often contradict each other.

ranges.summary: 50 foot (15.2 m) radius per level of experience; center placed up to 60 feet (18.3 m) away per level.

areaOfEffect.summary: 50 foot (15.2 m) radius per level of experience centered on the cast point.

duration.summary: Retroactive: instantly affects susceptible witnesses in area; covers events up to 2 minutes per level before activation. Preemptive: remains active 2 minutes per level; entrants during that time are affected.

save.summary: Save vs magic (15+) only if the witness suspects something is wrong and wants the truth. Those who do not want to believe auto-succumb without rolling.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Remembers a false, mundane explanation for what transpired — the illusion is permanent and real to them.
save.effectsOnSuccess: Sees through Ignorance is Bliss and remembers what actually happened.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Instantly affects susceptible witnesses still in the area; events up to 2 minutes per level before activation can be misremembered.
Talent stays active 2 minutes per level; anyone entering the radius during that time misremembers what they see.

rangeMechanics.summary: Cast up to 60 feet (18.3 m) away per level; 50 foot (15.2 m) radius per level of effect.
rangeMechanics.notes: Area radius scales 50 feet per level independently of cast range.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Permanent false mundane memory replaces supernatural events witnessed.
savingThrow.notes: No save for those who do not want to believe in monsters, magic, or scary things — they automatically succumb.

statusAlterations.summary: Affected mortals remember explainable, ordinary events instead of supernatural truth; false memories are permanent.

name: Infectious Control
description: This often-repulsive Talent allows the Nightbane to take over a person by infecting him with a portion of his body — spines, parasites, biomechanical probes, blood, and other manifestations vary by Morphus. Once infection completes (usually one melee round after the piece enters the victim), the subject is puppeteered for one melee round, then may save vs magic; on failure, control lasts 30 minutes with new saves every 10 minutes. While controlling a victim, the Nightbane experiences everything the victim does but splits attention between both bodies.

enhancement.summary: 20 P.P.E. per additional half hour of control. Memory search: 5 P.P.E. per piece of information extracted.

ppe.notes: Each memory search takes 1D6 minutes and yields one small piece of information; victim saves vs psionics to resist intrusion.

limitations.otherLimitations: Victims with M.E. 7 or less remain controlled for one hour at a time instead of 30 minutes.

duration.summary: Controlled for 30 minutes. Victims who have an M.E. of 7 or less are controlled for an hour at a time.

activationTime.summary: Usually takes one melee round from the time the piece of the Nightbane has entered the body of the victim.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Attacks/actions are divided evenly between the two bodies, no initiative bonus, and combat bonuses and skill performance are reduced by half unless control is relinquished.

ranges.summary: Infection delivery varies by Morphus (touch, ranged spines/needles, parasites, blood, biomechanical probes, etc.) — a portion of the Nightbane's body must enter the victim.

activationRequirements.summary: A portion of the Nightbane's body must enter the victim's body; infection usually completes one melee round later.

rangeMechanics.summary: Delivery range varies by Morphus manifestation; infection requires the Nightbane's tissue to enter the victim.
rangeMechanics.notes: Spines, needles, parasites, blood, biomechanical probes, and similar delivery methods are cosmetic variations with identical mechanics.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: While puppeteering: see, hear, and experience everything the victim does; control every word and movement. Relinquishing control removes caster penalties.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Victim is puppeteered for one mandatory melee round, then may save vs magic; on failure, control lasts 30 minutes (60 if victim M.E. ≤ 7) with new saves every 10 minutes.
Extract one small piece of information for 5 P.P.E.; takes 1D6 minutes. Victim saves vs psionics to resist intrusion.

statusAlterations.summary: Victim is puppeteered — controlled words and movements while the Nightbane experiences the victim's senses.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Control persists 30 minutes (60 if victim M.E. ≤ 7); new save every 10 minutes.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Control ends after the mandatory first melee round.
savingThrow.notes: Save allowed at end of the first round of control. Memory search uses a separate save vs psionics (5 P.P.E. per fact, 1D6 minutes each).

combatMechanics.notes: Mandatory one melee round of control before the first save. Caster splits attacks evenly between bodies unless control is relinquished.

save.summary: Save vs magic at the end of the first round of control; new saves every 10 minutes while control persists.
Separate save vs psionics to resist a memory-search intrusion.

name: Inferno Fist
description: One appendage becomes wreathed in flame, adding 2D6 damage to punches. Allows parrying magic weapons and energy weapons with the fiery hand (+1 to parry conventional melee weapons with the bare fiery hand; -3 to parry an incoming energy blast). Upon a successful strike or parry, Inferno Fist is used up and must be reactivated. Will not work with a held weapon, though objects in the flaming hand are not damaged.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. per activation for one melee round (15 seconds).

limitations.otherLimitations: Will not work in conjunction with a weapon of any sort. Objects held by the flaming appendage are not damaged. Inferno Fist discharges after one successful strike or parry. Only 2 P.P.E. may be spent on the activation of this Talent per level of experience.

ranges.summary: Self — melee combat / arm's length.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds); extinguishes if unused or if the Nightbane is knocked unconscious.

activationRequirements.summary: Bare fiery appendage only — cannot be used while wielding a weapon.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — melee / arm's length.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +1 to parry conventional melee weapons with the bare fiery hand; may parry magic weapons and energy weapons. Energy-blast parry: -3 penalty.

damage.summary: 2D6 added to punch damage while Inferno Fist is active.

combatMechanics.notes: Discharges on any successful strike or parry — must reactivate for further use. Extinguishes at end of melee round if unused or if knocked unconscious.

statusAlterations.summary: One appendage becomes wreathed in a nimbus of flame for the duration.

name: Iron Will
description: Strengthens the mind's natural defenses for Nightbane without psychic abilities. +3 to save vs mind control, psionic attacks, illusions, and pain; +4 to save vs possession. Negates skill performance penalties for being under duress or under fire. The user knows when psionic powers are being used against him and that something is trying to breach his mental defenses.

ppe.notes: One melee round (15 seconds) per level of experience per activation.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: User knows when psionic powers are used against him and that something is trying to breach his mental defenses.

physicalBonusesPenalties.notes: Negates skill performance penalties for being under duress or under fire for the duration.

statusAlterations.summary: Mind's natural defenses are strengthened against mental intrusion for the duration.

name: Jam Senses
description: Counters Hounds and similar Nightlands hunters by fouling their supernatural senses. Any targeted Hound that fails a save vs magic is temporarily robbed of its ability to sense and track the supernatural and its immunity to illusions and concealment powers. Affected creatures are also slightly disoriented (-1 initiative, strike, parry, and dodge). They can still hunt by sight, scent, and skill.

ppe.notes: Three P.P.E. per enemy targeted. P.P.E. is spent whether the creature fails to save or succeeds.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must have previously encountered a Hound, Hunter, or similar before selecting this Talent. One target may be affected per level of experience; activation cost is paid for each target. Only affects supernatural senses of Nightlands hunters — sight, scent, and conventional tracking skill are unaffected.

ranges.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute per level of the Nightbane's experience.

activationRequirements.summary: One Hound or similar Nightlands hunter per level of experience; pay 3 P.P.E. per target attempted.

save.summary: Targeted Hound saves vs magic (16+). Failure: loses supernatural tracking, illusion/concealment immunity, and suffers -1 initiative, strike, parry, and dodge.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Temporarily robbed of supernatural sense/tracking and immunity to illusions and concealment; -1 initiative, strike, parry, and dodge.

rangeMechanics.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) per level of experience.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Temporarily robbed of supernatural tracking senses, immunity to illusions/concealment, and suffers -1 to initiative, strike, parry, and dodge.
savingThrow.notes: Affected creatures can still hunt by sight, scent, and skill.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Applies only on failed save vs magic.

statusAlterations.summary: Fouls supernatural senses of targeted Hounds and similar Nightlands hunters.

name: Killing Spree
description: While active, half the remaining P.P.E. of any being the user kills is stolen and temporarily added to their own pool for the duration. Stolen P.P.E. is immediately available to fuel Talents and spells. Absorbing kill-P.P.E. can trigger a murderous frenzy: for every 50 P.P.E. absorbed within one hour, cumulative 10% chance of a mental endurance save (15+) or the Nightbane loses control. Frenzy rules vary by alignment; good/selfish characters rarely harm non-combatants, evil characters may slaughter indiscriminately.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per melee round (15 seconds). Normally lasts one melee round; during frenzy, P.P.E. keeps burning to sustain Killing Spree until end conditions are met.

limitations.otherLimitations: User must deliver the killing blow to absorb P.P.E. — not merely participate. P.P.E. from kills by others or at range cannot be absorbed. During frenzy: cannot share P.P.E., resists siphoning (+1 save), cannot draw from ley lines/nexuses, cannot cast spells, no ranged strike bonuses, offensive Talents only (or mobility to reach targets). Frenzy ends when stolen P.P.E. is exhausted, 20 minutes pass without victims while hunting, user is subdued and prevented from killing for 20 minutes, or user is slain.

ranges.summary: Self for the ability; touch (killing blow) to absorb victim P.P.E.

duration.summary: Normally one melee round per activation; extends automatically during frenzy until end conditions are met.
duration.notes: Special duration while in killing frenzy — P.P.E. continues burning each melee round.

activationRequirements.summary: User must personally deliver the killing blow to absorb the victim's remaining P.P.E.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self; P.P.E. absorption requires the killing blow at touch range.

save.summary: For every 50 P.P.E. absorbed within one hour, cumulative 10% chance of mental endurance save (15+) or the user enters killing frenzy.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Loses control — semi-intelligent predator seeking the next kill (alignment governs who may be harmed).

vampiricResourceRecovery.notes: Half the victim's remaining P.P.E. is stolen and immediately available for Talents and spells while Killing Spree is active.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Frenzy only: +2 save vs Horror Factor; immune to pain; will not pull punches. -4 Perception, -3 parry/dodge/disarm/roll with impact, -35% skills except hunting/killing skills and W.P.s.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Frenzy penalties only. Hunting/tracking and killing skills (including W.P.s) are exempt from the skill penalty.

alignmentGatedEffects.summary: In frenzy, will not harm non-combatants (selfish unlikely to); assaults enemies or anyone who attacks first until they flee or die.
In frenzy, may slaughter every living being encountered; henchmen/allies/friends spared only if they stay out of the way and do not interfere.

savingThrow.notes: Cumulative 10% chance per 50 P.P.E. absorbed within one hour to require a save or enter frenzy.

combatMechanics.notes: Frenzy weapons: hands, feet, claws, fangs, simple melee weapons. Offensive Talents only, or mobility Talents to reach unavailable targets.

statusAlterations.summary: While active, kill-absorbed P.P.E. fuels further violence; failed endurance saves trigger a murderous killing frenzy.

name: Launch
description: Hurls a single visible inanimate object at a visible target in a telekinesis-like fashion (+2 to strike; no other strike bonuses). Weapon projectiles inflict normal damage +1D6. Objects over 30 lbs have a 33% chance to knock down human-sized opponents (lose initiative and one attack). Requires a gesture — arm wave or nod/glance toward the target.

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. per melee round. Each Launch counts as one of the user's melee attacks.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one object hurled at a time. Object and target must be visible. No fine manipulation, suspended hold, or parrying with launched objects. Objects under 20 lbs (9 kg) may be parried at -2; larger must be dodged at -1.

ranges.summary: 50 feet (15.2 m) + 20 feet (6.1 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round per activation; each Launch counts as one of the user's melee attacks.

activationRequirements.summary: One visible inanimate object and one visible target; requires a gesture (arm wave or nod/glance toward the target).

rangeMechanics.summary: 50 feet (15.2 m) + 20 feet (6.1 m) per level of experience.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +2 to strike only — no other strike bonuses apply. Thrown weapons inflict normal weapon damage +1D6.

damageModifiersNote: Thrown weapons are flung point or blade first: usual weapon damage +1D6 force bonus.

damage.summary: 1D4 S.D.C. (up to 2 lbs / 0.9 kg)
1D6 S.D.C. (up to 5 lbs / 2.25 kg)
2D4 S.D.C. (up to 10 lbs / 4.5 kg)
3D4 S.D.C. (up to 20 lbs / 9 kg)
3D6 S.D.C. (up to 30 lbs / 13.5 kg)
4D6 S.D.C. (up to 40 lbs / 18 kg)
5D6 S.D.C. (up to 50 lbs / 22.5 kg)
6D6 S.D.C. (up to 60 lbs / 27 kg — maximum)

statusEffects.summary: 33% chance of knocking down a human-sized opponent if weight exceeds 30 lbs. Target loses initiative and one attack.

combatMechanics.notes: Objects under 20 lbs (9 kg) may be parried at -2; larger must be dodged at -1. No fine manipulation, suspended hold, or parrying with launched objects.

name: Leap
description: Propels the user beyond normal jumping ability. Each P.P.E. spent adds 5 feet (1.5 m) to vertical jump height or 10 feet (3 m) to horizontal distance. Combined with a flying tackle or leap kick, adds 1D6 S.D.C. damage. May be used to dodge by leaping aside (usual dodge bonuses apply). Grants Judge Distance 60% + 3% per level for difficult jumps (moving targets or distances over 50 feet).

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per Talent-assisted leap. Each 1 P.P.E. beyond the leap cost adds 5 ft vertical or 10 ft horizontal. Maximum 4 P.P.E. per level of experience per activation.

limitations.otherLimitations: When attacking via Leap: -1 to strike per 20 feet (6.1 m) covered and costs an additional attack. Distance and P.P.E. cost are chosen at activation and cannot be altered mid-leap. Failed Judge Distance roll: target undershot or overshot (G.M. determines consequences).

ranges.summary: +5 feet (1.5 m) vertical or +10 feet (3 m) horizontal per P.P.E. spent beyond normal jump limits (up to 4 P.P.E. per level).

duration.summary: Instant.

activationRequirements.summary: Distance and P.P.E. cost must be chosen at activation; cannot be altered mid-leap.

rangeMechanics.summary: +5 feet (1.5 m) vertical or +10 feet (3 m) horizontal per P.P.E. spent.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Roll Judge Distance for difficult jumps (moving targets or distances over 50 feet / 15.2 m).

jumpDistanceScaling.summary: 1 P.P.E. = +5 ft (1.5 m) vertical or +10 ft (3 m) horizontal beyond the character's normal jump.

damage.summary: 1D6 S.D.C. added to a flying tackle or leap kick combined with Leap.

combatMechanics.notes: Leap dodge uses usual dodge bonuses. Attack leap: -1 strike per 20 feet (6.1 m) of distance covered; costs one additional attack.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Propels the user beyond normal jumping ability; may dodge by leaping aside.

grantedSkills.notes: 60% + 3% per additional level for difficult jumps after practice.

name: Leave No Trace
description: Supernatural counter to modern forensic detection. Basic mode prevents trace evidence (hairs, fingerprints, blood droplets) and eliminates tracks or footprints. Boosted mode greatly obscures the user to electronic cameras, sensors, and listening devices — blurry security footage, garbled wire taps, and laser trip wires/motion detectors/microwave fences triggering only 01–30% of the time.

activationTiers.summary: Basic: no trace evidence, no tracks or footprints — 6 P.P.E.
Boosted: basic effects plus electronic/camera/sensor obscurement — 10 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: Double duration in the Nightlands.

self.notes: Basic or boosted on self in Facade or Morphus.

others.notes: Basic benefits only; boosted Leave No Trace cannot be used on another individual.

limitations.otherLimitations: Boosted mode or bestowing basic benefits on another requires Morphus form. Boosted cannot target anyone other than self.

ranges.summary: Self or other by touch.

duration.summary: Five minutes per level of the user's experience (doubled in the Nightlands).

activationRequirements.summary: Touch required to bestow basic benefits on another person (Morphus only). Self-use needs no touch.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Touch for others; self is automatic

rangeMechanics.summary: Self or other by touch.

powerModes.effectsDescription: No trace evidence (hairs, fingerprints, blood droplets); no tracks or footprints. 6 P.P.E.

Basic effects plus obscurement to electronic cameras, sensors, and listening devices — blurry footage, garbled wire taps, laser trip wires/motion detectors/microwave fences trigger only 01–30% of the time. 10 P.P.E.; self only.

statusAlterations.summary: Beneficiary leaves no forensic trace and (boosted) is greatly obscured to electronic surveillance.

combatMechanics.notes: Boosted mode: security cameras capture only blurry indistinguishable images; motion and microwave sensors trip 01–30% of the time.

name: Lightning Rider
description: The Nightbane becomes a being of pure electrical energy. Unless extra P.P.E. is spent on voltage, the charge is weak and inflicts no damage — but the character can travel through conductive materials at enormous speeds. On activation the Nightbane vanishes in a flash and leaps onto the nearest conductor; without one, he becomes a small pool of sparks or crackling lightning ball moving at speed 8. Telephone lines, power grids, and modem traffic (especially with Computer Operations or Computer Programming) enable world-spanning travel in minutes.

enhancement.summary: 10 P.P.E. per each additional minute of travel. Optional voltage: 1D6 S.D.C. per 5 P.P.E. per attack.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. per minute of travel after a one-melee-round activation concentration. Without extra voltage P.P.E., the electrical form inflicts no damage.

ranges.summary: Self — electrical form travels through available conductive paths (telephone lines, power grids, Internet/modem traffic, exposed metal).

duration.summary: 10 P.P.E. per minute of travel after one-melee-round activation.

activationTime.summary: One melee round of concentration to activate.

activationRequirements.summary: One melee round of concentration; on activation the Nightbane vanishes in a flash and leaps onto the nearest conductive material.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — transit through conductive infrastructure at enormous speed.
rangeMechanics.notes: World-spanning travel in minutes via telephone lines, power grids, and modem traffic. Without a conductor, becomes a spark pool or lightning ball at speed 8.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Travel through conductive materials (metal, wires, telephone lines, power grids, modem traffic) at enormous speeds — world-spanning travel in minutes.
mobilityBlocks.notes: Vanishes in a flash and leaps onto the nearest conductor. Computer Operations aids navigating the information superhighway; Computer Programming enables advanced hacking-style travel.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Without a nearby conductor: becomes a small pool of sparks or crackling lightning ball crawling at speed 8.

offensiveDamage: 1D6 S.D.C. per 5 P.P.E. per attack (optional voltage)

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: While in electrical form, characters with Computer Operations can navigate modem/internet traffic; Computer Programming enables advanced hacking-style travel through digital infrastructure.

damage.summary: Optional voltage: 1D6 S.D.C. per 5 P.P.E. per attack. Base form inflicts no damage.

combatMechanics.notes: 8 S.D.C. fries most electronics (radios, computers, etc.). Tempest-hardened systems survive up to 40 S.D.C. before being disabled.

statusAlterations.summary: Transforms into pure electrical energy — harmless at base voltage, capable of traversing conductive infrastructure.

notes: Without extra voltage P.P.E., the electrical form is harmless. Travel speed through conductors is enormous; fallback spark/ball form moves at speed 8.

name: Livewire
description: Commands a length of cable or wire like a serpent — ripping free from devices, walls, or telephone poles and writhing through the air at speed 36 (23 mph/37 kph). The wire has two attacks per melee and is +3 to strike, entangle, and dodge. If severed or destroyed, the enchantment ends. Alternatively, the user controls it by mind and gesture, but each attack counts as one of their own melee attacks.

activationTiers.summary: Light/residential cable: 5 P.P.E. — ~6 S.D.C./foot, 1D6 whip, shock 1D6+2 once (no stun), break free P.S. 18 (2 attacks)
High-gauge cable (power lines, tow cables, etc.): 8 P.P.E. — ~20 S.D.C./foot, 2D6 whip, shock 2D6+4 (01–33% stun), break free P.S. 30 (2 attacks)
Heavy-duty cable (transmission lines, mooring cable, etc.): 12 P.P.E. — ~50 S.D.C./foot, 3D6 whip, shock +1D6 (01–66% stun 2D4 melees), break free P.S. 40 (3 attacks)

limitations.otherLimitations: Must clearly see both wire and target, or know each location with certainty. Stun chance and duration halved against supernatural opponents. Save vs electricity (13+, P.E. applies) halves shock damage and prevents stun. Damage not increased vs Nightbane-vulnerable targets. Untangling while animated is impossible — break free or cut loose only.

ranges.summary: Animates a 20 foot (6.1 m) length of wire/cable up to 100 feet (30.5 m) away.

duration.summary: 30 seconds (2 melee rounds) per level of experience.

activationRequirements.summary: Must clearly see both the wire/cable and intended target, or know each location with certainty.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Line of sight or certain knowledge of wire and target

rangeMechanics.summary: Animates a 20 foot (6.1 m) length of cable up to 100 feet (30.5 m) away.
rangeMechanics.notes: Animated segment is 20 feet (6.1 m) long.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Wire moves at speed 36 (23 mph / 37 kph) with 2 attacks/melee and +3 strike, entangle, and dodge on its own.
User directs each whip or entangle; each attack counts as one of the user's melee attacks.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Animated cable writhing through the air at speed 36 (23 mph / 37 kph).

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Autonomous mode only. Enchantment ends if the cable is severed or destroyed.

save.summary: Shock victims save vs electricity (13+, P.E. bonus applies). Success: half damage, no stun. Failure: full damage and stunned.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Full shock damage; stunned (-2 attacks that melee round, -3 all combat rolls for 1D4 melee rounds; heavy cable may stun 2D4 melees).

damage.summary: Light/residential (~6 S.D.C./ft): 1D6 whip; shock 1D6+2 once if electrified (no stun)
High-gauge (~20 S.D.C./ft): 2D6 whip; shock 2D6+4 (01–33% stun)
Heavy-duty (~50 S.D.C./ft): 3D6 whip; shock +1D6 (01–66% stun for 2D4 melees)

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Full shock damage and stun penalties.
savingThrow.notes: Stun chance and duration halved against supernatural opponents.

combatMechanics.notes: Break free: P.S. 18 / 30 / 40 (light / high-gauge / heavy) using 2 or 3 melee attacks. Untangling while animated is impossible.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Stunned victims: -2 attacks that melee round and -3 all combat rolls for 1D4 melee rounds (heavy cable: 2D4 melees). Stun halved vs supernatural opponents.

statusAlterations.summary: Cable or wire rips free and behaves like a serpent under the Nightbane's command.

name: Mage Siphon
description: Attempts to steal P.P.E. from a visible target within range. One P.P.E. channeled into activation steals 1D6 P.P.E. (two points steals 2D6, and so on). Stolen P.P.E. is immediately usable but can only be held for five minutes per level of experience. Magic or psionic barriers (Magic Armor, Shadow Shield, etc.) block siphoning and instead take damage equal to the P.P.E. that would have been stolen. Metal plate mail and similar spell-casting interference halves P.P.E. stolen.

enhancement.summary: One P.P.E. spent steals 1D6 P.P.E. from the target (up to the per-level cap).

ppe.notes: Maximum 2 P.P.E. spent per level of experience per use (e.g. 2 P.P.E. at level 1 = up to 2D6 stolen).

limitations.otherLimitations: Target must be visible and within range. Barriers absorb siphon damage instead (no save). Spell-casting armor halves amount stolen.

ranges.summary: 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: Instant siphon; stolen P.P.E. retained up to five minutes per level of experience.

activationRequirements.summary: Target must be visible and within range. Maximum 2 P.P.E. spent per level of experience on each use.

rangeMechanics.summary: 10 feet (3 m) per level; line of sight required.

save.summary: Save vs magic (14+). Success prevents P.P.E. from being siphoned. Does not apply when a barrier intercepts the siphon.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: 1D6 P.P.E. stolen per P.P.E. channeled into activation (plate mail and similar halves amount stolen).

vampiricResourceRecovery.triggerSummary: On successful activation when the target fails their save and no barrier blocks the siphon.

temporaryResourcePools.summary: Stolen P.P.E. is immediately usable but can only be held for five minutes per level of experience.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Loses 1D6 P.P.E. per P.P.E. channeled (halved if target wears spell-casting-interfering armor such as metal plate mail).
savingThrow.notes: Magic or psionic barriers block siphoning and take damage equal to the P.P.E. that would have been stolen (1D6, 2D6, etc.) — no save.

combatMechanics.notes: Barriers include Magic Armor, Shadow Shield, and similar effects.

damageModifiersNote: Metal plate mail and armor that interferes with spell casting halves P.P.E. stolen.

name: Mass Transit
description: Teleports the user between Earth and the Nightlands (and vice versa), bringing people and/or supplies along. Highly sought by Factions for troop and supply movements. The user must travel with everyone/everything transported, know the exact destination, and may only reach the corresponding point in the other dimension or a Nightlands location previously visited. Can grab specific people and objects within the radius of influence.

enhancement.summary: Ten P.P.E. per additional individual or equivalent object (satchel, trunk, crate, or container up to 400 lbs / 180 kg).

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. transports self; +10 P.P.E. per additional passenger or cargo item.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only the corresponding point in the other dimension or a previously visited Nightlands location. User must accompany all passengers/cargo and know the exact destination. P.P.E. is expended even if unwilling targets resist and remain behind.

ranges.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius at 7th level, +10 feet (3 m) radius per level beyond 7th.

areaOfEffect.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius at 7th level (+10 feet / 3 m per level above 7).

duration.summary: Must concentrate one melee round (15 seconds) per 10 P.P.E. expended before the dimensional jump occurs.
duration.notes: Concentration scales with total P.P.E. spent on the activation.

activationRequirements.summary: User must know the exact destination and accompany all passengers/cargo. Destination must be the corresponding point in the other dimension or a previously visited Nightlands location.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Known exact destination; user travels with cargo

activationTime.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) of concentration per 10 P.P.E. expended before the jump.
activationTime.notes: Total concentration time scales with P.P.E. spent (e.g. 30 P.P.E. = 3 melee rounds).

rangeMechanics.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius at 7th level, +10 feet (3 m) per level above 7.

passengerLimits.summary: 20 P.P.E. transports self; +10 P.P.E. per additional individual or equivalent cargo (satchel, trunk, crate, or container up to 400 lbs / 180 kg). User must travel with everyone/everything transported.

save.summary: Unwilling passengers/targets save vs magic (12+). Success avoids being taken; P.P.E. is still spent.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Target is transported with the user to the known destination.
save.effectsOnSuccess: Target remains behind; P.P.E. still expended.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Unwilling passenger or target is taken along in the dimensional jump.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Target resists transport; P.P.E. is still spent.

combatMechanics.notes: User can selectively grab specific people and objects within the radius of influence.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Dimensional teleport between Earth and the Nightlands (or vice versa).
mobilityBlocks.notes: Only corresponding points or previously visited Nightlands locations.

statusAlterations.summary: Mass dimensional transit — user and selected passengers/cargo vanish and reappear at the known destination.

name: Mimic Spell
description: Reactivates the short-lived metaphysical echo of a recently observed spell, capturing and copying it at the same level it was originally cast. The Nightbane may direct it at a new target and adjust damage within the spell's usual parameters — useful for Sorcerers and Mystics to recast favorites quickly or use spells they do not know. Cannot mimic rituals or component-dependent spells unless the user has the required components. When multiple spells were cast in the same area, only the last spell can be mimicked.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate plus the mimicked spell's usual P.P.E. cost (e.g. a 4 P.P.E. spell costs 14 total; a 25 P.P.E. spell costs 35 total).

limitations.otherLimitations: Magic-using Nightbane may select at 2nd level; all others require 4th level. Spell trace must have been cast within the last six minutes (trace fades entirely within 1D4×10 minutes). Captured spell must be cast within one melee round (15 seconds) or the mimic is lost.

ranges.summary: May recreate a spell cast within 200 feet (61 m).

duration.summary: Mimicked spell must be cast within one melee round. Mimicked spell duration matches the original unless deliberately altered.

notes: Range, duration, damage, and effects of the reactivated spell match the original observed casting.

activationRequirements.summary: Must have observed the spell's casting or its tell-tale effects. Cannot mimic rituals or component-dependent spells unless the user possesses the required components. When multiple spells were cast in the same area, only the last spell can be mimicked.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Observed spell trace within time window

retroactiveWindow.notes: Spell trace too weak to mimic after six minutes; all trace is gone within 1D4×10 minutes.

activationTime.summary: Captured mimicked spell must be cast within one melee round (15 seconds) or the mimic is lost.

rangeMechanics.summary: Recreates a spell cast within 200 feet (61 m) of the mimicker.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Reactivates the observed spell at the same level it was originally cast. The mimicker casts it as if from himself — choosing target, regulating damage, and adjusting within the spell's usual parameters. Range, duration, damage, and effects match the original unless deliberately altered.

statusAlterations.summary: Captures and reactivates the short-lived metaphysical echo of a recently observed spell.

name: Mimic Talent
description: Duplicates the effects of almost any Nightbane Talent witnessed within 100 feet — one activation only, fully under the mimicker's control (e.g. a mimicked Doorway need not open to the same exit unless chosen). Must meet all requirements of the copied Talent (level gates, Morphus restrictions, HP/P.E. costs, etc.). Power level and capabilities match the observed user, not the mimicker's level. Duration of the mimicked Talent is halved.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate Mimic Talent plus the usual P.P.E. costs required to use the Talent being mimicked.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only duplicate the last Talent witnessed with one's own eyes. Must mimic within 60 seconds (four melee rounds) of witnessing it. Mimicked Talent is used once; cannot duplicate again unless witnessed and mimicked anew.

ranges.summary: Must witness the original Talent within 100 feet (30.5 m); line of sight required. Mimicked Talent range is roughly what was observed.

duration.summary: Mimicked Talent is instantly available; its own duration is half that of the original.

activationRequirements.summary: Must witness the last Talent used with one's own eyes within 100 feet (30.5 m) and line of sight. Must meet all requirements of the copied Talent (level gates, Morphus restrictions, HP/P.E. costs, etc.). Single use — cannot duplicate again unless witnessed and mimicked anew.

retroactiveWindow.notes: Four melee rounds after witnessing the original Talent use.

rangeMechanics.summary: Must witness the original Talent within 100 feet (30.5 m); line of sight required. Mimicked Talent range is roughly what was observed.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Single-use duplicate of the witnessed Nightbane Talent, fully under the mimicker's control (e.g. a mimicked Doorway need not open to the same exit unless chosen). Power level and capabilities match the observed user, not the mimicker's level. Duration of the mimicked Talent is halved.

attributeCost.summary: Must pay any HP or P.E. attribute costs required by the copied Talent in addition to Mimic Talent and mimicked Talent P.P.E. costs.
attributeCost.notes: All level gates, Morphus restrictions, and resource costs of the copied Talent apply to the mimicker.

statusAlterations.summary: Duplicates the effects of the last witnessed Nightbane Talent for one activation.

name: Mirror Search
description: Prerequisite: Mirror Sight. The Nightbane sees through the mirror closest to the person or object sought. This area-effect Talent scans a 10 mile (16 km) radius (more with additional P.P.E.) for the target. The nearest mirror may not show the target directly — only the closest area and mirror to it. Searching for a kidnapped friend might reveal a hotel bathroom (towels showing the hotel name) or a room mirror showing the friend bound and interrogated by thugs and a Hound.

enhancement.summary: +5 P.P.E. per additional 10 miles (16 km).

duration.summary: Instant vision through the closest mirror to the sought target.

activationRequirements.summary: Requires Mirror Sight. Declares a specific person or object to locate; reveals the mirror closest to that target within the scan radius.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Named person or object search target

rangeAndArea.summary: Scans a 10 mile (16 km) radius; +10 miles (16 km) per additional 5 P.P.E.

rangeMechanics.summary: Area scan centered on the Nightbane: 10 mile (16 km) radius base; +10 miles (16 km) per +5 P.P.E.
rangeMechanics.notes: Does not guarantee the nearest mirror shows the target directly — only the closest area and mirror to the sought person or object.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: See through the mirror closest to the sought person or object. Vision is image-only (no sound), like looking through a closed window — may show adjacent rooms, hallways, or views outside nearby windows.

combatMechanics.notes: Covering a mirror (blanket, towel, etc.) blocks effective use — only the covering is seen. Painting a mirror blocks passage and use as a window. Nightlords and similarly empowered beings can still enter through covered mirrors.

statusAlterations.summary: Remote mirror scrying — reveals the nearest mirror view to the sought target within the scanned area.

areaOfEffect.summary: Effectively scans a 10 mile (16 km) radius.

notes: Shows images only — no sound, like looking through a closed window. Covering a mirror blocks sight/search (only the covering is seen); painting a mirror blocks passage and use as a window. Nightlords and similarly empowered beings can still enter through covered mirrors.

name: Mirror Sense
description: Senses mirrors in the vicinity connected to the Nightlands with pinpoint precision — how many, their direction, and distance. Concentrating on a single mirror reveals whether it links to the Nightlands and shows a blurry, shadowy image of the Nightlands side (a shadowy reflection of the room the user stands in, not the surrounding Nightlands). Any Nightbane can tell by looking at a mirror whether it connects; this Talent senses them across a wide area before approaching.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only detects mirrors with a Nightlands counterpart — not other dimensional portals, breaches, or anomalies. Identifies which buildings contain linked mirrors; occupants are not necessarily hostile.

ranges.summary: 500 foot (152.4 m) radius + 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Five minutes per level of the Nightbane.

rangeMechanics.summary: 500 foot (152.4 m) radius + 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience.

activationRequirements.summary: Detects only mirrors with a Nightlands counterpart — not other dimensional portals, breaches, or anomalies.

activationTime.summary: Instant area sense; concentrating a few seconds on a single mirror reveals whether it links to the Nightlands.
activationTime.notes: Nightlands preview shows a blurry, shadowy reflection of the room the user stands in — not the surrounding Nightlands or anyone there.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Pinpoint sense of how many Nightlands-connected mirrors are in range, their direction, and distance to each. Concentrating on one mirror reveals its Nightlands link and a shadowy preview of the mirrored room on the other side.

statusAlterations.summary: Supernatural detection of Nightlands-linked mirrors across a wide area before approaching their surfaces.

name: Mirror Shield
description: Summons a mirrored protective shield (buckler to medium size) strong as steel with 75 S.D.C. (damaged only if targeted). Provides +3 to parry — no other parry bonuses apply except from a high P.P. attribute. Negates the normal penalty to parry projectiles, allowing parries against bullets, magical fireballs, and energy blasts. Parried attacks may strike nearby people or objects for half damage.

limitations.otherLimitations: Shield remains on the user's arm or in hand for the duration — cannot be dropped or passed to another, but may be canceled at any time.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Ten minutes per level of experience or until the shield is destroyed.

createdObjects.name: Mirrored Shield
createdObjects.damageRules: Only takes damage if specifically targeted. Can parry energy and magical projectile attacks without normal penalties. Parried attacks may hit nearby objects/people for half damage.

activationRequirements.summary: Shield remains on the user's arm or in hand for the duration — cannot be dropped or passed to another. May be canceled at any time.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — shield summoned on the user's arm or hand.

parry.summary: +3 to parry while using the shield.

combatMechanics.notes: Negates the normal penalty to parry projectiles — can parry bullets, magical fireballs, and energy blasts. No other parry bonuses apply except those from a high P.P. attribute. Parried attacks may strike nearby people or objects for half damage.

reflectionMechanics.triggerCondition: Parried projectile or energy attacks leveled at the user (bullets, magical fireballs, energy blasts).
reflectionMechanics.notes: Deflects attacks via parry without the usual projectile penalty. Parried attacks may hit nearby people or objects for half damage — use carefully to avoid collateral harm.

statusAlterations.summary: Summons a mirrored shield (buckler to medium size) strong as steel, affixed to arm or hand until duration ends, destroyed, or canceled.

name: Mirror Sight
description: Enables the Nightbane to see the other side of a mirror into the Nightlands, and vice versa, as if it were a window. A mirror or highly reflective surface (glass, polished metal, a quiet body of water) must be available. The character can only see what the mirror image faces in the Nightlands, and vice versa. Some Nightlands creatures may sense they are being watched and cross over to confront the observer.

limitations.otherLimitations: Requires a mirror or highly reflective surface (glass, polished metal, a quiet body of water).

ranges.summary: Requires a local mirror or highly reflective surface — see only what the mirror image faces on the other side.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience.

activationRequirements.summary: A mirror or highly reflective surface must be available. Covering a mirror blocks sight (only the covering is seen); painting a mirror blocks passage and use as a window.

rangeMechanics.summary: Through an available mirror or reflective surface into the Nightlands (and vice versa), as if it were a window.
rangeMechanics.notes: Can only see what the mirror image faces on the Nightlands side, and vice versa. Vision is image-only — no sound.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: See the other side of a mirror into the Nightlands, and vice versa, as if looking through a closed window.

combatMechanics.notes: Some Nightlands creatures may sense they are being watched and cross over to confront or attack the observer (see Nightlands section). Covering a mirror blocks effective use; painting blocks passage and window use. Nightlords and similarly empowered beings can still enter through covered mirrors.

statusAlterations.summary: Mirror-window scrying into or out of the Nightlands through a reflective surface.

notes: Shows images only — no sound. Covering a mirror blocks sight (only the covering is seen); painting a mirror blocks passage and use as a window. Nightlords and similarly empowered beings can still enter through covered mirrors.

name: Nega
description: Counters and terminates any spell, Talent, or other supernatural ability fueled by P.P.E. that is directed at the Nightbane — including area effects, though only the user is spared. Does not work against true psionic attacks (I.S.P.). Once active, affecting powers are nullified; the user must spend P.P.E. matching the incoming power's cost or it resolves normally.

baseActivation.relativeToSummary: Spend the same P.P.E. as the spell or Talent directed at the user.

ppe.notes: Can protect up to two others by touch at once for the P.P.E. cost of one (or self plus one other). Automatically lapses when the user reaches 0 P.P.E.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot be selected until 4th level. Only negates abilities fueled by psychic or magic energy (P.P.E., etc.) — not true psionic attacks (I.S.P.). The user cannot choose what is countered; this includes their own active powers. If the user lacks enough P.P.E. to match the incoming power, it works normally.

ranges.summary: Self; or expanded to a maximum of 2 feet (0.6 m) from the user at 4th level, plus 2 feet (0.6 m) at levels 6, 8, 10, 12, and 14. Up to two others may be protected by touch.

duration.summary: Determined by the activation cost paid; automatically lapses when the user reaches 0 P.P.E.

notes: Seekers and Nocturnes often assign a Nega user to teams hunting mages or powerful supernatural beings.

activationRequirements.summary: Must spend P.P.E. matching the spell or Talent directed at the user; insufficient P.P.E. lets the incoming power work normally. Cannot choose what is countered — includes the user's own active powers. Only negates abilities fueled by psychic or magic energy (P.P.E.) — not true psionic attacks (I.S.P.).

rangeMechanics.summary: Self, or expanded to 2 feet (0.6 m) from the user at 4th level plus 2 feet (0.6 m) at levels 6, 8, 10, 12, and 14. Up to two others protected by touch at the P.P.E. cost of one.
rangeMechanics.notes: Area of effect expandable per level milestones; touch protection for up to two others (or self plus one other).

rules: Once Nega is active, directed supernatural abilities fueled by P.P.E. affecting the protected subject are nullified when activation cost is matched.
Area-effect spells or Talents spare only the Nega user (and up to two touched allies); others in the area are not protected.
Not applicable to true psionic attacks (I.S.P.).

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Counters and terminates spells, Talents, and supernatural abilities directed at the user or touched allies.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Cannot select what is countered; user's own active powers may be nullified. Automatically lapses when the user reaches 0 P.P.E.

statusAlterations.summary: Nullifies incoming supernatural effects fueled by P.P.E. once activation cost is paid.

combatMechanics.notes: Protect up to two others by touch at once, or self and one other by touch, for the P.P.E. cost of one. Area-effect negation protects only the Nega user and touched allies, not the full area.

name: Nightbringer
description: Absorbs light around the Nightbane, creating an area of unnatural darkness equal to 10 feet (3 m) in diameter per every 2 P.P.E. spent. The power also absorbs energy attacks, except Shadow Blast, Darkwhip, and other darkness-based attacks and magic. People caught in the darkness suffer combat and perception penalties (halved for creatures with nightvision).

enhancement.summary: Each additional 2 P.P.E. increases the diameter of the darkness by 10 feet (3 m).

ppe.notes: Minimum 2 P.P.E. to activate; base 2 P.P.E. yields 10 feet (3 m) diameter darkness.

ranges.summary: Self-centered — unnatural darkness radiates from the Nightbane.

areaOfEffect.summary: 10 feet (3 m) in diameter per every 2 P.P.E. spent.

rangeAndArea.summary: 10 foot (3 m) diameter unnatural darkness per 2 P.P.E. spent (minimum 2 P.P.E.).

rangeMechanics.summary: Self-centered darkness: 10 foot (3 m) diameter per 2 P.P.E. spent.
rangeMechanics.notes: Minimum 2 P.P.E. activation yields a 10 foot (3 m) diameter area.

environmentalConstructs.summary: Unnatural darkness that absorbs ambient light and most energy attacks.

rules: Absorbs energy attacks directed into the darkness, except Shadow Blast, Darkwhip, and other darkness-based attacks and magic.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Penalties apply to people caught in the unnatural darkness. Halved for creatures with nightvision.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Strike, parry, dodge, and perception penalties are halved (-3 strike/parry/dodge, -2 perception).

combatMechanics.notes: Darkness absorbs energy attacks except Shadow Blast, Darkwhip, and other darkness-based attacks and magic. Occupants suffer -6 strike/parry/dodge and -4 perception (halved with nightvision).

statusAlterations.summary: Creates an expanding field of unnatural darkness that absorbs light and most energy attacks.

name: Open Channel
description: Opens to the supernatural to sense one chosen species of being in the area — while simultaneously alerting every member of that species to the Nightbane's presence.

limitations.otherLimitations: Unavailable until 2nd level. Does not indicate exact location or numbers — only rough groupings (one or two, a few [3–6], several [7–16], many [17+], great many [50+]). 'Minions of the Nightlords' or 'creatures of the Nightlands' is too broad; choose a specific species (Hounds, Hunters, Guardians, Wampyrs, Namtars, a Night Prince, etc.). Nightbane in Morphus (not Facade) can also be sensed. Each sensed individual also senses the user. To sense a different species, must reactivate. The user may restrict sensing to a 100 foot (30.5 m) radius or expand in 100 foot (30.5 m) increments up to full range.

ranges.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience; may be restricted to 100 foot increments.

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience

notes: A gut-feeling sense for the user; a dead giveaway to beings like Hounds and Hunters that a Nightbane is nearby.

activationRequirements.summary: Upon activation, choose one specific supernatural species (Hounds, Hunters, Guardians, Wampyrs, Namtars, a Night Prince, Nightbane in Morphus, etc.). 'Minions of the Nightlords' or 'creatures of the Nightlands' is too broad. To sense a different species, must reactivate and spend new P.P.E.

rangeMechanics.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience; may restrict to 100 foot (30.5 m) radius or expand in 100 foot increments up to full range.
rangeMechanics.notes: User may limit sensing to 100 foot increments.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Gut-feeling sense of presence for the chosen species — one or two, a few (3–6), several (7–16), many (17+), or great many (50+). Rough near/far sense only; no exact count or location. Nightbane in Morphus (not Facade) are detectable as a species choice.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Every sensed individual of the chosen species also senses the user's presence — they know a Nightbane is lurking nearby. May alert or provoke a hunt.

statusAlterations.summary: Mutual supernatural detection — user senses one chosen species while each member of that species senses the user.

combatMechanics.notes: Does not detect magic users or psychics. Does not indicate exact location or numbers.

name: Phantom Limb
description: Summons translucent, ghostly appendages (extra arms, claws, tentacles, etc.) that function as additional limbs — useful for combat, carrying loads, repairs, and climbing.

enhancement.summary: Summon additional phantom limbs.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per phantom limb activated. One limb per 3 levels of experience (1 at 3rd, 2 at 6th, 3 at 9th, 4 at 12th, 5 at 15th).

limitations.otherLimitations: One limb per 3 levels of experience (1 at 3rd, 2 at 6th, 3 at 9th, 4 at 12th, 5 at 15th). If meant for complex tasks like operating a computer or shooting, limbs must have digits and an opposable thumb.

ranges.summary: Self

activationTime.summary: One melee action

duration.summary: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience

stepScaling.summary: +1 to parry and entangle per summoned limb.

createdObjects.name: Phantom Limb
createdObjects.damageRules: Has Supernatural P.S. of 16. Normal hand to hand strikes deal 1D6 damage.

activationRequirements.summary: One phantom limb per 3 levels of experience (1 at 3rd, 2 at 6th, 3 at 9th, 4 at 12th, 5 at 15th). Limbs for operating a computer or shooting must have digits and an opposable thumb.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self only.

damage.summary: 1D6 damage from normal hand-to-hand strikes (Supernatural P.S. 16 per limb).

combatMechanics.notes: Each extra limb adds +1 to parry and entangle. Each pair adds +1 attack per melee round.

statusAlterations.summary: Summons translucent, faintly glowing ectoplasmic appendages — extra arms, claws, tentacles, etc.

name: Piece Work
description: Removes a portion of the Nightbane's own flesh — an eye, ear, finger, toe, or other sensory part. The detached piece can hear, see, and feel its surroundings (limited by placement) but cannot move or communicate. The user learns nothing until reattachment; on reattach, he remembers everything the piece experienced. Each piece has 10 S.D.C. and 4 Hit Points.

enhancement.summary: 6 P.P.E. per hour after the first hour the piece remains detached.

ppe.notes: 8 P.P.E. to detach; +6 P.P.E. each hour after the first or the piece vanishes (body part can regenerate, but gathered information is lost).

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot learn from the detached piece until reattached. If maintenance P.P.E. is not paid, the piece disappears — the body part regenerates but any information it gathered is lost. If the piece is burned, eaten, or destroyed, it ceases to exist with the same effect.

ranges.summary: Self — detach and place a body part at a chosen location.

duration.summary: First hour included at activation; +6 P.P.E. per additional hour or the piece vanishes.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — detach a sensory appendage and place it as a stationary spy.

objectsAffected.summary: Detached piece is placed at a chosen location; senses are limited by placement (line of sight, concealment, etc.).

createdObjects.targetSummary: Detached sensory body part or appendage
createdObjects.name: Spy Piece
createdObjects.notes: Can hear, see, and feel surroundings but cannot move or communicate. S.D.C. and Hit Points cannot regenerate until the piece is destroyed or reattached.

rules: Each detached piece has 10 S.D.C. and 4 Hit Points.
Piece S.D.C. and Hit Points cannot regenerate until the piece is destroyed or reattached.
If the piece is burned, eaten, or otherwise destroyed, it ceases to exist — the body part regenerates but gathered information is lost.

combatMechanics.notes: On reattachment, the Nightbane remembers everything the piece experienced as if he had been there. No information is available until reattachment. Unpaid hourly maintenance or piece destruction ends surveillance with no recovered memories.

statusAlterations.summary: Removes an eye, ear, finger, toe, or other sensory flesh and leaves it as a passive spy device.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Cannot learn anything from the detached piece until reattachment. Missing-piece maintenance costs 6 P.P.E. per hour after the first or the piece vanishes and information is lost.

name: Pipe Bomb
description: Coalesces dark energy into a form-fitting shell resembling Shadow Shield. Absorbs incoming damage into the shell's S.D.C.; on depletion or command it explodes outward in a 15 foot (4.6 m) sphere — only the wearer is unscathed.

enhancement.summary: Each 2 P.P.E. adds 10 S.D.C. to the shell and +1D6+1 explosive damage.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. = 10 S.D.C. and 1D6+1 damage; four = 20 S.D.C. and 2D6+2; eight = 40 S.D.C. and 4D6+4; twelve = 60 S.D.C. and 6D6+6. Total spend capped at 4 P.P.E. per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot select blast targets — everyone and everything in the blast radius takes damage. Intentionally detonating after activation counts as two separate melee attacks. If duration ends without detonation, the shell crumbles away without exploding. Can be cancelled at any time. Only other Nightbane can tell Pipe Bomb apart from Shadow Shield.

ranges.summary: Self (15 foot / 4.6 m blast radius on detonation).

areaOfEffect.summary: 15 foot (4.6 m) blast radius on detonation.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience, until S.D.C. is depleted, willfully detonated, or cancelled.

createdObjects.name: Pipe Bomb Shield

offensiveDamageScalingByPpeStep.stepDefinition: Each 2 P.P.E. adds 10 S.D.C. to the shell and +1D6+1 to the explosive blast.

createdObjects.damageRules: Incoming damage is absorbed by the shell's S.D.C. first. On detonation, only the wearer is unscathed; blast damage is not reduced by prior shell damage.

activationRequirements.summary: Activating and then intentionally detonating counts as two separate melee attacks. Cannot select blast targets. May be cancelled at any time.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self; 15 foot (4.6 m) blast radius on detonation.

rules: Incoming damage is absorbed by the Pipe Bomb shell's S.D.C. before the wearer takes harm.
On detonation, blast damage equals 1D6+1 S.D.C. per 2 P.P.E. spent at activation — not reduced by prior shell damage.

damage.summary: 1D6+1 S.D.C. per 2 P.P.E. spent at activation (e.g. 4 P.P.E. = 2D6+2, 8 = 4D6+4, 12 = 6D6+6).

combatMechanics.notes: Only the wearer is unscathed on detonation. Everyone and everything in the 15 foot blast radius takes full damage — friend, foe, or bystander. P.P.E. capped at 4 per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Form-fitting dark-energy shell visually indistinguishable from Shadow Shield to non-Nightbane.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Cannot select who is harmed by the blast. If duration expires without detonation, shell crumbles away without exploding.

name: Pocket Change
description: Conjures physical currency out of thin air by picturing the amount required — coins or paper notes that pass any forgery inspection, but vanish when the duration ends.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per dollar (or local equivalent) created. The Nightbane chooses coin or note form up to the P.P.E. spent.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must picture the currency to create and be familiar with its denominations and appearance — an example to focus on is best. Money vanishes completely when the duration ends.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience, then vanishes completely.

activationRequirements.summary: Must close eyes, picture the amount required, and be familiar with the currency's denominations and appearance — an example to focus on is best.

activationTime.summary: Close eyes and picture the desired amount before activation.
activationTime.notes: One physical dollar (or local equivalent) appears in hand per P.P.E. spent; user chooses coin or note form up to the P.P.E. spent.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — currency appears in the user's hand.

createdObjects.name: Conjured Currency
createdObjects.notes: Passes any inspection or forgery test. Vanishes completely when duration ends.

statusAlterations.summary: Conjures indistinguishable physical currency that vanishes when the Talent expires.

name: Premonition
description: Grants feelings and visions about the future, often at unexpected times (when the G.M. decides) or at moments of crisis. Feelings warn that something good, bad, or dangerous will happen soon, at a particular place, or to a particular person — without dreams or images. Visions are symbolic images and/or short dream sequences with accompanying feelings; omens may be cryptic but should hint at things to come (e.g. a shadowed killer ending on a stylized eagle that later ties to a tattoo, gang symbol, corporate logo, or other clue).

activationTiers.summary: Active attempt to sense the future: 2 P.P.E.
Spontaneous premonition (when the G.M. triggers at crisis or narrative moment): 2 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: 2 P.P.E. to activate when actively sensing the future; 2 P.P.E. when a spontaneous premonition occurs.

limitations.otherLimitations: Premonitions may occur spontaneously or when the Nightbane actively attempts to sense the future.

ranges.summary: Self — future sense with no fixed range.

activationRequirements.summary: May trigger spontaneously (G.M. decides timing) or when the Nightbane actively attempts to sense the future.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — no fixed spatial range; omens may reference places, people, or events the G.M. deems relevant.

powerModes.effectsDescription: A gut sense that something good, bad, or dangerous will happen soon, at a particular place, or to a particular person — no dreams or images.
Symbolic images and/or short dream sequences with accompanying feelings. Omens may be cryptic but should hint at things to come (tattoos, logos, gang symbols, etc.).

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Grants narrative forewarning through feelings or symbolic visions — timing and content are ultimately G.M. adjudicated.

combatMechanics.notes: Not a combat power. Visions are omens, not certainties — symbols may point to individuals, groups, corporations, or other clues rather than a single culprit.

statusAlterations.summary: Receives feelings or symbolic visions about future danger, opportunity, or crisis.

name: Raven Wings
description: Manifests large black wings of solid shadow (or a winged flying apparatus) for magically powered flight. The wings have no physical substance, do not impair movement, and cannot be damaged.

enhancement.summary: Extend sustained flight.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. for 10 minutes of flight; four P.P.E. per each additional 10 minutes sustained.

limitations.otherLimitations: No altitude limit, but air gets too thin to breathe around 20,000 feet (6,096 m). Flight speed limited to 20 mph (32 kph) per level of experience. No piloting skill required; no special combat bonuses other than +2 to dodge while in flight.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: 10 minutes base; +10 minutes per 4 P.P.E. spent after activation.

activationRequirements.summary: Morphus only. No piloting skill required. Air too thin to breathe around 20,000 feet (6,096 m).

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — magically powered flight.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Magically powered flight at 20 mph (32 kph) per level of experience.
mobilityBlocks.notes: No altitude limit beyond breathing constraints. Wings have no physical substance, do not impair movement, and cannot be damaged.

rules: Flight apparatus/wings cannot be damaged.
No strain on the Nightbane other than exposure to the elements.

combatMechanics.notes: No special combat bonuses other than +2 to dodge while in flight. No piloting skill required to maneuver.

statusAlterations.summary: Large black wings of solid shadow (raven to eagle) or a winged flying apparatus — intangible and undamageable.

name: Reality Mooring
description: Anchors incorporeal beings to the physical plane — forcing spirits, ghosts, Entities, and Astral Beings to manifest, flee, or become vulnerable to physical harm within its area of effect.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per melee round of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot be selected before 4th level. Incorporeal beings must save vs magic (16+) or manifest physically or leave for 1D6 days. Astral Projectors are snapped back to their bodies and cannot Astral Project for 24 hours. Ghosts and Entities incapable of manifesting are forced outside the area and cannot return for 1D6 days. Characters within the area cannot Astral Project or become intangible for the duration. On the Astral Plane, only Astral Travelers are forced back. Forced physical manifestation has Horror Factor 15; affected beings suffer -2 Perception, -2 initiative, -1 attack, -2 to all combat rolls, and -20% to skills for 1D4 melee rounds. Use with caution — survivors may lash out in fear.

ranges.summary: 15 feet (4.6 m) radius per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds).

save.summary: 16 or better to save vs magic; failure forces manifestation on the physical plane or banishment for 1D6 days.

statusEffects.summary: Incorporeal beings that fail the save are forced to become physical or retreat to their home plane. If forced physical, they suffer penalties.

rangeMechanics.summary: 15 feet (4.6 m) radius per level of experience.

activationTime.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) per 15 P.P.E. spent.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Forced physical manifestation only; lasts 1D4 melee rounds. Horror Factor 15 when ethereal creatures discover they are no longer invisible and intangible.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Forced to manifest physically (vulnerable to capture and physical harm) or banished for 1D6 days. Astral Projectors snapped back — cannot Astral Project for 24 hours.

combatMechanics.notes: Ghosts and Entities incapable of manifesting are forced outside the area (cannot return 1D6 days). Characters in the area cannot Astral Project or become intangible. On the Astral Plane, only Astral Travelers are forced back. Survivors may lash out in fear.

statusAlterations.summary: Anchors incorporeal beings to physical reality within the radius — combatting astral spies and intangible foes.

name: Reshape Facade
description: Although contemptuously called the Facade, the Nightbane's human form is as unique and real as the Morphus. Temporarily alters the Facade — features, hair color, even size. More extensive changes cost more P.P.E. Imitating a specific person is possible but difficult (20% + I.Q. bonus + 3% per level of experience).

enhancement.summary: 4 P.P.E. per additional hour the disguise is desired.

ppe.notes: More extensive Facade changes cost more P.P.E. beyond the base activation.

limitations.otherLimitations: Impersonating a particular person: 20% + I.Q. bonus + 3% per level of experience to pass convincingly.

ranges.summary: Self — alters the Nightbane's Facade form only.

duration.summary: One hour base; +1 hour per 4 additional P.P.E.

activationRequirements.summary: Facade form only. More extensive Facade changes cost additional P.P.E. beyond the base activation.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — temporary Facade reshaping.

grantedSkills.notes: 20% base + I.Q. bonus + 3% per level of experience to pass convincingly when imitating a particular individual.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Temporarily alters Facade features, hair color, and even size. Extent of change affects P.P.E. cost beyond base activation.

combatMechanics.notes: Impersonating a specific person requires a convincing-disguise roll: 20% + I.Q. bonus + 3% per level. Cosmetic Facade changes without impersonation do not require the roll.

statusAlterations.summary: Temporarily reshapes the Facade — altered features, hair, and human form size until duration ends.

name: See Truth
description: Each activation reveals the true nature of one person or object. Against Nightbane, the user sees Facade and Morphus superimposed. Alignment is visible (humans and Nightbane; reading a Nightbane's alignment counts as a separate use). Also reveals strong magic emanations, undead/vampires, serious illness, and strong emotions (e.g. hatred behind a smile) — aspects of the target's soul and personality.

enhancement.summary: Every additional 4 P.P.E. raises the save number by +1 (maximum of 18, for 24 P.P.E.).

limitations.otherLimitations: Line of sight up to 300 feet (91 m). Reading a Nightbane's alignment requires a separate activation from seeing their true forms.

ranges.summary: Line of sight up to 300 feet (91 m) away.

save.summary: Save against magic (12 or higher) to resist revealing the truth.

duration.summary: Instant — one person or object per activation.

activationRequirements.summary: One person or object per activation within line of sight. Reading a Nightbane's alignment requires a separate activation from seeing their true forms.

rangeMechanics.summary: Line of sight up to 300 feet (91 m) away.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Reveals true nature of one target: Nightbane Facade and Morphus superimposed, alignment (humans and Nightbane), strong magic emanations, undead/vampires, serious illness, and strong emotions — aspects of the soul and personality.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: The Nightbane sees the target's true nature.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Target conceals their true nature from See Truth.
savingThrow.notes: Raise the save DC by +1 per 4 additional P.P.E. spent (maximum 18 at 24 P.P.E. total including base activation).

statusAlterations.summary: Supernatural truth-sight — pierces disguises, hidden forms, and soul-level emanations on one target.

name: Severance
description: Temporarily shuts down the supernatural aura — undetectable to Nightbane, psychics, mages, and paranormal sensors. Defeats Hounds, Hunters, Great Hounds, Soul Trackers, and other Nightbane's Sense Nightbane; aura reads as an ordinary human. While active, no other Talents or magic (Mystic/Sorcerer) can be used; the user feels cut off from the metaphysical world (unsettling for Mystics).

limitations.otherLimitations: Self only. Cannot use other Talents or cast magic while Severance is active.

ranges.summary: Self only.

duration.summary: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self only — supernatural aura suppression.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Undetectable to Nightbane, psychics, mages, and supernatural creatures that sense paranormal auras. Defeats Hound, Hunter, Great Hound, and Soul Tracker tracking, and other Nightbane's Sense Nightbane. Aura reads as an ordinary human.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Cannot use any other Talents or cast magic (Mystic/Sorcerer) while Severance is active. Feels cut off from the metaphysical world — unsettling for Mystics attuned to the supernatural.

combatMechanics.notes: Passive concealment only — no direct combat effect while the aura is suppressed.

statusAlterations.summary: Supernatural aura shut down — appears to be an ordinary human to paranormal senses and trackers.

name: Shadow Blast
description: Fires a black energy bolt dealing 1D4 S.D.C. per point of P.P.E. spent — 10 P.P.E. yields a 1D4×10 S.D.C. bolt, and so on.

enhancement.summary: 1 P.P.E. per every 1D4 points of damage.

ppe.notes: Total P.P.E. spent on a single bolt cannot exceed 4 per level of experience.

duration.summary: Instant — fires an almost instantaneous black energy bolt.

activationRequirements.summary: Total P.P.E. spent on a single bolt cannot exceed 4 per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: 500 feet (152 m); line of sight.

attacksAndDamage.summary: 1D4 S.D.C. per 1 P.P.E. spent — e.g. 10 P.P.E. yields 1D4×10 S.D.C.

damage.summary: 1D4 S.D.C. per 1 P.P.E. spent on the bolt (e.g. 4 P.P.E. at level 1 = 1D4×4; 20 P.P.E. at level 5 = 1D4×20).

combatMechanics.notes: Maximum 4 P.P.E. per level of experience on a single Shadow Blast.

statusAlterations.summary: Fires a black energy bolt of darkness-based force.

damageModifiersNote: Damage is 1D4 S.D.C. per 1 P.P.E. spent.

name: Shadow Pockets
description: Access a mini-dimension for storing small objects — reach into clothing pockets or pull items from a shadow like sleight of hand. Usable in Facade or Morphus; each placement or retrieval costs P.P.E. Objects rest in limbo indefinitely and only the owner can retrieve them, even while sleeping or unconscious.

enhancement.summary: 8 P.P.E. to place or retrieve one object.

ppe.notes: 8 P.P.E. per object placed into or retrieved from a Shadow Pocket.

limitations.otherLimitations: Capacity: 5 lbs (2.25 kg) per level; no single object over 15 lbs (6.75 kg). One melee attack/action per place or retrieve. Killed in Morphus: stored objects appear where the user stood. Killed in Facade: objects remain in the shadow dimension and are lost.

ranges.summary: Self — personal shadow-pocket mini-dimension.
