duration.summary: Stored objects remain in limbo indefinitely until retrieved.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — access a personal mini-dimension for object storage.

activationTime.summary: One melee attack/action per object placed into or retrieved from a pocket.

objectsAffected.summary: Small objects only — up to 15 lbs (6.75 kg) per item; total capacity 5 lbs (2.25 kg) per level of experience.

passengerLimits.notes: Objects only — not living beings. P.P.E. spent per item placed or retrieved.

environmentalConstructs.summary: Personal shadow-pocket mini-dimension — objects held in limbo and retrievable only by the Nightbane who stored them.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Killed in Morphus form: stored objects appear where the user last stood. Killed in Facade form: objects remain in the shadow dimension and are presumably lost forever.

combatMechanics.notes: 8 P.P.E. per object placed or retrieved. Objects persist without ill effect while the owner sleeps or is unconscious.

statusAlterations.summary: May reach into clothing pockets or draw items from shadows — cosmetic presentation only.

name: Shadow Shield
description: Creates a force field of dark energy around the Nightbane, providing 20 S.D.C. per 2 P.P.E. spent. The Shadow Shield completely surrounds the character, protecting from toxic gases, disease, punches, bullets, heat, fire, and similar dangers; the shield absorbs damage until depleted.

enhancement.summary: 2 P.P.E. for each additional 20 S.D.C.

maxEnhancementPpePerActivation.notes: Maximum of 4 P.P.E. spent per level of experience, granting a maximum of 40 S.D.C. per level.

ppe.notes: 2 P.P.E. to activate with 20 S.D.C.; cannot exceed 40 S.D.C. per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: Maximum 40 S.D.C. per level of experience (4 P.P.E. per level cap on total spend).

duration.summary: 30 minutes per level of experience or until all the S.D.C. of the shadow shield is used up.

ranges.summary: Self — completely surrounds the Nightbane.

activationRequirements.summary: 2 P.P.E. activates a 20 S.D.C. shield; +2 P.P.E. per additional 20 S.D.C. Total spend capped at 4 P.P.E. per level (40 S.D.C. per level maximum).

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — full-body dark-energy force field.

createdObjects.name: Shadow Shield
createdObjects.damageRules: Completely surrounds the character. Absorbs toxic gases, disease, punches, bullets, heat, fire, and similar dangers — the shield takes all damage until depleted.
createdObjects.notes: 20 S.D.C. per 2 P.P.E. spent. Maximum 40 S.D.C. per level of experience (4 P.P.E. per level cap on total spend).

rules: Shield absorbs toxic gases, disease, punches, bullets, heat, fire, and similar physical/environmental dangers.
The shield — not the Nightbane — takes damage from absorbed attacks until its S.D.C. is depleted or duration ends.

combatMechanics.notes: Full-body dark-energy force field. Easily confused with Anti-Arcane's black-light aura or Pipe Bomb's similar shell.

statusAlterations.summary: Enveloped in a force field of dark energy that absorbs harm until depleted.

name: Shadow Slide
description: The Nightbane must be in Morphus form. Becomes an insubstantial two-dimensional shadow — like a heat mirage on pavement — that sinks into the earth and fades away. Slithers along walls or ground, virtually invisible, sliding through any opening no matter how narrow. Clothing and small personal effects transform automatically.

enhancement.summary: 10 P.P.E. per each minute the Talent is maintained. Carried objects: 2 P.P.E. per pound (0.45 kg) of weight.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. to activate; 10 P.P.E. per each additional minute maintained.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only air-tight containers prevent the shadow-slider from passing through.

ranges.summary: Self — insubstantial shadow form.

duration.summary: 10 P.P.E. per minute after activation.

activationRequirements.summary: Morphus form only. Clothing and small personal effects transform automatically. Extra cargo: 2 P.P.E. per pound (0.45 kg). Only air-tight containers block passage.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — slithers along walls or ground through any non-air-tight opening.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Slithers along walls or across the ground as a virtually invisible two-dimensional shadow.
mobilityBlocks.notes: Can pass through any opening, no matter how narrow. Air-tight containers prevent entry.

statusEffects.name: Shadow Form
statusEffects.summary: Insubstantial, flickering two-dimensional shadow — virtually invisible.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Slides through narrow openings while clothing and small personal effects remain transformed. Carried objects cost 2 P.P.E. per pound (0.45 kg).

combatMechanics.notes: Only air-tight containers prevent the shadow-slider from passing through.

statusAlterations.summary: Becomes an insubstantial shadow that sinks into surfaces and fades from normal sight.

name: Shadow Weapon
description: Creates a one-piece melee weapon of dark energy (sword, knife, axe, polearm, pick, mace, morning star, etc. — not bows or modern weapons with moving parts). Weapon type is chosen when the Talent is first acquired and never changes. Substantial, can parry, cannot be thrown; vanishes if it leaves the user's hands. W.P. bonuses apply when the weapon matches the proficiency.

enhancement.summary: 5 P.P.E. for each subsequent melee round of use.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. for the first melee round; 5 P.P.E. per each additional melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: One weapon type chosen at acquisition; cannot be thrown. Does 50% greater damage to Night Princes and Nightlords.

ranges.summary: Melee — manifested dark-energy weapon.

duration.summary: 10 P.P.E. for the first melee round; 5 P.P.E. per each additional melee round.

rangeMechanics.summary: Melee reach of the manifested Shadow Weapon.

activationRequirements.summary: Weapon type chosen when the Talent is first acquired — one-piece melee only (sword, knife, axe, pole arm, pick, mace, morning star, etc.; not bows or modern weapons with moving parts).

attacksAndDamage.summary: 2D6 base damage +1D6 at levels 6, 9, 12, and 15. +50% damage vs Night Princes and Nightlords. Cannot be thrown.

bonusDamageVsNightlandsDenizens.summary: +50% damage vs Night Princes and Nightlords.

createdObjects.name: Shadow Weapon
createdObjects.damageRules: 2D6 +1D6 at levels 6, 9, 12, and 15; +50% vs Night Princes and Nightlords. W.P. bonuses apply when weapon matches proficiency.
createdObjects.notes: Substantial one-piece melee weapon of dark energy. Can parry; cannot be thrown; vanishes if it leaves the user's hands.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Weapon Proficiency bonuses apply when the manifested weapon type matches an applicable W.P.

damage.summary: 2D6 base; +1D6 at levels 6, 9, 12, and 15.

combatMechanics.notes: Can parry. Cannot be thrown — weapon disappears if it leaves the Nightbane's hands.

statusAlterations.summary: Manifests a personal dark-energy melee weapon — appearance fixed when the Talent is first acquired.

damageModifiersNote: +50% damage vs Night Princes and Nightlords.

name: Sharing the Flesh
description: A strange, horrific healing Talent. The Nightbane touches an injured person to magically transfer their wounds, injury, and pain to himself. The patient falls into a coma or sleep for 1D4 melee rounds, then awakens completely well. Wounds often appear spontaneously on the Nightbane as the subject heals. He may transfer as much damage as he can tolerate — a painful sacrifice; Nightbane heal rapidly compared to humans.

activationTiers.name: Critically injured healing
activationTiers.effectDescription: Stabilize critically injured/comatose victims: expend 24 P.P.E. and 100 S.D.C./Hit Points to bring the patient to 2D6 Hit Points. The Nightbane falls into a coma for 2D4×10 minutes.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must be performed as a voluntary act of sacrifice. Critical healing costs 100 S.D.C./Hit Points from the Nightbane; if fewer than 100 are available, he can still perform it but dies in the process.

ranges.summary: Touch

healing.summary: Transfers damage to the Nightbane, fully healing the target. Critically injured targets are brought to 2D6 Hit Points.

duration.summary: Patient falls into coma or sleep for 1D4 melee rounds, then awakens completely well.

activationRequirements.summary: Touch an injured person. Must be a voluntary act of sacrifice performed in Morphus form.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch — injured person must be contacted directly.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 8 P.P.E. — transfer as much of the patient's damage as the Nightbane can tolerate. Patient sleeps 1D4 melee rounds, then awakens fully healed; wounds appear on the Nightbane.
24 P.P.E. plus 100 S.D.C./Hit Points from the Nightbane — stabilizes critically injured/comatose victims to 2D6 Hit Points. Nightbane falls into a coma for 2D4×10 minutes.

endWhenThresholdsMet.notes: Critical stabilization: Nightbane remains in a healing coma for 2D4×10 minutes after the procedure.

recoveryStateUntilThreshold.notes: Applies to the Nightbane healer after critical stabilization, not the patient.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Nightbane takes transferred wounds and pain; heals at normal Nightbane rates. Critical mode costs 100 S.D.C./Hit Points — if fewer than 100 are available, the Nightbane still performs the healing but dies in the process.

attributeCost.summary: Critical stabilization: 100 S.D.C. and/or Hit Points from the Nightbane (fatal if combined total below 100).

combatMechanics.notes: Patient is helpless in sleep/coma for 1D4 melee rounds during normal healing. Critical patients are brought to 2D6 Hit Points; healer enters 2D4×10 minute coma.

statusAlterations.summary: Gruesome wound transfer — injuries spontaneously appear on the Nightbane as the patient heals.

name: Skill Stealer
description: Temporarily robs a sentient target of a single chosen skill and transfers that knowledge to the Nightbane at the victim's skill percentage.

cannotAffect: Animals or monsters with instincts and innate abilities
limitations.otherLimitations: Unavailable until 3rd level. Only one skill per use. A newly stolen skill replaces any previously stolen skill still active. Works on physical skills and Weapon Proficiencies but grants only the skill knowledge — not associated combat bonuses (strike, parry, dodge, extra attacks, etc.); the Nightbane's P.P. and other attribute bonuses may still apply. Can target a skill the Nightbane already knows to deny the enemy its use or borrow a higher percentage from the victim.

ranges.summary: Touch or up to 40 feet (12.2 m) away; intended victim must be visible.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience; may be returned early at any time.

save.summary: Save vs magic 14 or better to resist having the skill stolen.

effectsOnSuccessfulSave.notes: Target retains their skill and the Nightbane gains nothing.

notes: Uses the victim's exact skill percentage. If the Nightbane already knows the skill at a higher percentage, the stolen version is ignored and the user's own level applies.

activationRequirements.summary: Choose one skill from a visible sentient target (living or undead). Only one skill per use; a new theft replaces any still-active stolen skill.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch or up to 40 feet (12.2 m); line of sight to victim required.

skillsGranted.notes: Victim's exact percentage; no W.P. combat bonuses (strike, parry, dodge, extra attacks). Nightbane P.P. and attribute bonuses may still apply. If Nightbane already knows the skill at a higher percentage, uses own level instead.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Victim completely forgets the stolen skill for the duration; knowledge transfers to the Nightbane. May return the skill early at any time.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Chosen skill stolen at victim's percentage.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Target retains skill; Nightbane gains nothing.

combatMechanics.notes: Works on physical skills and Weapon Proficiencies. Does not affect animals or monsters with instincts and innate abilities only.

statusAlterations.summary: Temporarily robs a single skill from a sentient target and transfers it to the Nightbane.

name: Sky Writer
description: Manipulates clouds to write sky messages visible within 5 miles (8 km), shift cloud cover, or tint and darken clouds for dramatic effect — conjuring thin clouds on clear days if needed.

activationTiers.summary: Write in the sky: 1 P.P.E. per 6 letters or symbols.
Shift a cloud's position: 5 P.P.E.
Darken or tint clouds within range: 10 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: Double all P.P.E. costs if clouds must be conjured rather than manipulated from existing ones.

limitations.otherLimitations: Tinting and darkening create dramatic storm-like appearance only — no actual weather change.

ranges.summary: Any cloud within a 5 mile (8 km) radius; sky writing visible to everyone in that radius.

duration.summary: Two minutes (8 melee rounds) per level of experience.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 1 P.P.E. per 6 letters or symbols; visible to everyone within 5 miles (8 km). Thin clouds can be conjured on clear days.
5 P.P.E. to slowly move clouds — block sun/moonlight, obscure airborne foes, or create animated cloud art.
10 P.P.E. Dramatic storm-like appearance only — no actual weather change.

rangeMechanics.summary: Any cloud within 5 miles (8 km); sky writing visible throughout that radius.

activationRequirements.summary: Double all P.P.E. costs if clouds must be conjured rather than manipulated from existing ones.

environmentalConstructs.summary: Manipulates existing clouds or conjures thin clouds on clear days for writing, repositioning, or tinting.

statusAlterations.summary: Cloud manipulation for sky messaging, cover, or dramatic storm-like appearance.

name: Solar Flare
description: Summons a small orb of magical sunlight usable as a 5 foot (1.5 m) lantern, a laser-like bolt (+3 to strike), or an aerial flash against creatures vulnerable to sunlight.

activationTiers.summary: Laser bolt at one target: 5D6 damage (double vs vampires and Shadow creatures); flare vanishes on impact.
Aerial sunlight flash: 2D6 damage per 4 P.P.E. spent to all sunlight-vulnerable creatures within 100 feet (30.5 m) of the user.

ppe.notes: Maximum 4 P.P.E. per level of experience per activation. Lantern mode keeps sunlight-vulnerable creatures out of the 5 foot radius but does not harm them. Aerial flash: no damage to targets shielded indoors or in darkness; Horror Factor 12 to sunlight-vulnerable creatures at night; visible 2D4 miles (3.2–12.8 km); +2 initiative for user and allies first melee while others are surprised.

limitations.otherLimitations: Not available for selection until 3rd level. Only 4 P.P.E. per level of experience may be spent per activation.

ranges.summary: Lantern: 5 foot (1.5 m) radius. Bolt or launch: up to 1,000 feet (305 m). Aerial flash illuminates 1,000 foot (305 m) diameter; damages sunlight-vulnerable targets within 100 feet (30.5 m) of the user.

duration.summary: Lantern: up to one melee round (15 seconds) per level of experience. Near-instant once fired or launched.

offensiveDamage: 5D6 (laser bolt); 2D6 per 4 P.P.E. (aerial flash vs sunlight-vulnerable)
notes: Unlike Globe of Daylight, this Talent has offensive applications. Aerial flash may blind light-sensitive creatures; does not blind otherwise.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 5 foot (1.5 m) radius light; bright enough to read by. Keeps sunlight-vulnerable creatures from entering the area but does not harm them. Up to one melee round per level.
Launch at one target up to 1,000 feet (305 m): 5D6 damage (+3 to strike). Double damage to vampires and Shadow creatures. Flare vanishes on impact.

Launch into air: 1,000 foot (305 m) diameter lit as bright as daytime for 1D4 seconds (one melee attack). 2D6 damage per 4 P.P.E. to sunlight-vulnerable creatures within 100 feet of user. HF 12 at night; visible 2D4 miles; +2 initiative user/allies first melee.

rangeMechanics.summary: Lantern: 5 foot radius. Bolt/launch: 1,000 feet (305 m). Aerial flash damage: sunlight-vulnerable within 100 feet of user.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +3 to strike when firing the laser bolt.

damage.summary: Laser bolt: 5D6 (double vs vampires and Shadow creatures).
Aerial flash: 2D6 per 4 P.P.E. to sunlight-vulnerable within 100 feet; no damage if shielded indoors or in darkness away from windows.

combatMechanics.notes: Aerial flash counts as one melee attack. May blind light-sensitive creatures. Horror Factor 12 to sunlight-vulnerable at night. Maximum 4 P.P.E. per level per activation.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Laser bolt deals double damage. Aerial flash damages sunlight-vulnerable creatures only.

bonusesOnAlliedTargets.notes: Aerial flash at night: +2 initiative for user and allies first melee round while others are surprised. Signal visible 2D4 miles (3.2–12.8 km).

statusAlterations.summary: Summons a small orb of magical sunlight — usable as lantern, laser bolt, or aerial flash.

name: Soul Shield
description: Protects against mental attacks, probes, and the See Truth Talent. Acts like a psychic mind block — most magical or psionic probes cannot intrude. While active, mind-control and domination powers automatically fail. Probing psychics see only a pool of utter darkness and must save vs psionics every melee round to break free; until they do, they stare vacantly and can take no other action.

limitations.otherLimitations: Mind control and domination effects automatically fail while the shield is up.

duration.summary: One hour.

save.summary: Psychics or mind readers who probe the shielded character must save vs psionics every melee round to escape the darkness trap.

ranges.summary: Self — mental protection aura.

activationRequirements.summary: Protects the Nightbane's mind while active. Mind control and domination automatically fail.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — blocks intrusion into the protected Nightbane's mind.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Acts like a psychic mind block — blocks most magical and psionic probes, See Truth, and automatically negates mind control or domination attempts.

rules: Mind control and domination powers automatically fail while Soul Shield is active.
Most magical or psionic mental probes cannot intrude; See Truth cannot pierce the shield.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Probing psychic is trapped in utter darkness — stares vacantly and can take no other action until breaking free.
savingThrow.notes: Save required every melee round while attempting to read a Soul Shielded mind.

combatMechanics.notes: Intruding mind readers see only a pool of utter darkness.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Probing psychics who fail to break free stare vacantly into space and can take no other action each melee round until they save.

statusEffects.name: Soul Shield
statusEffects.summary: Psychic mind block — intruders perceive only a pool of utter darkness.

statusAlterations.summary: Mind shielded against probes, See Truth, and domination effects for one hour.

name: Speed Darkling
description: Incredible speed equal to Spd × 10 mph (16 km). Bonuses: +1 attack per melee, +1 initiative, +4 auto-dodge (dodging does not use a melee action). When the effect ends, the user is exhausted for 1D6 melee rounds.

enhancement.summary: 5 P.P.E. for each additional minute of use.

ppe.notes: 15 P.P.E. for the first minute; 5 P.P.E. per each additional minute.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — enhanced ground speed.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Running speed equals Spd attribute × 10 mph (16 km).

autoDodge.summary: +4 auto-dodge while active; dodging does not consume a melee action.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Auto-dodge does not consume a melee action while Speed Darkling is active.

combatMechanics.notes: While active: +1 attack per melee, +1 initiative, +4 auto-dodge (free dodge). Afterburn lasts 1D6 melee rounds when the effect ends.

statusAlterations.summary: Moves at incredible superhuman speed for the duration.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Afterburn for 1D6 melee rounds when duration ends: normal Spd halved, −1 attack per melee, acts last (no initiative), −2 strike/parry/dodge.

name: Splittin' Image
description: Separates Facade and Morphus into two bodies — the Nightbane becomes two people. Aware of both sides but consciousness must focus on one form at a time (switch viewpoints at will). The focused form acts normally; the other lapses into a trance. May merge back into one body at will.

enhancement.summary: 10 P.P.E. per each additional hour (may be spent up front or as needed).

ppe.notes: 20 P.P.E. for the first hour; additional hours cost 10 P.P.E. each.

limitations.otherLimitations: Activated from Morphus only. The character can merge back into one body at will.

activation.notes: While active, Facade and Morphus exist as separate bodies; consciousness focuses on one at a time.

duration.summary: One or more hours depending on the amount of P.P.E. expended.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Penalties apply to the trance-like form the character is not currently focusing on.

ranges.summary: Splits the Nightbane into separate Facade and Morphus bodies at their current locations.

activationRequirements.summary: Activate from Morphus form. Consciousness must focus on one body at a time; may switch viewpoints or merge back into one body at will.

rangeMechanics.summary: Two simultaneous bodies — Facade and Morphus — wherever each form was when split.
rangeMechanics.notes: Both bodies share awareness; only the focused body acts without trance penalties.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Facade and Morphus exist as separate people simultaneously. The focused form acts normally; the unfocused form lapses into a trance.

powerModes.effectsDescription: The body receiving consciousness acts normally.
Unfocused body: -2 to all combat and Perception Rolls, half attacks per melee, -40% to all skills.

combatMechanics.notes: Switch consciousness between bodies at will. Merge back into one body at will. Unfocused body suffers trance penalties.

statusAlterations.summary: Nightbane exists as two people — Facade and Morphus separated until merged.

name: Storm Maker
description: Controls weather — rain, wind, and temperature. More extreme changes and larger areas cost more P.P.E. Two or more Nightbane who know Storm Maker may combine efforts and P.P.E. for greater effect. Effects take 1D6×10 minutes to manifest, last one hour, then require the same P.P.E. spend to sustain or fade away.

activationTiers.name: Minor change
activationTiers.effectDescription: Raise wind up to 5 mph (8 km), alter cloudiness, intensify existing rain, alter overall temperature by 4°F.

activationTiers.name: Moderate change
activationTiers.effectDescription: Start rain on an overcast day, turn a downpour into a storm or vice versa, alter overall temperature by 10°F.

activationTiers.name: Major change
activationTiers.effectDescription: Start rain during a clear sunny day, stop a downpour, create a major thunder/electrical storm during rain, alter overall temperature by 20°F.

activationTiers.name: Extreme change
activationTiers.effectDescription: Create fog, start hail or electrical storm on a clear day, turn a storm into a sunny day, turn a thunderstorm into a tropical storm (70 mph/112 km winds), alter overall temperature by 30°F.

activationTiers.name: Total upheaval
activationTiers.effectDescription: Start a hurricane or tornado (90+ mph/144 km winds), create a blinding snowstorm, dissipate a hurricane or terrible storm, alter overall temperature by 50°F.

enhancement.summary: Each additional mile of radius doubles the tier P.P.E. cost (e.g. moderate change: 10 P.P.E./mile, 20 for 2 miles, 40 for 3 miles, 80 for 4 miles).

ppe.notes: Activation P.P.E. varies by tier. At the end of each hour, spend the same amount again or the effect fades.

limitations.otherLimitations: Two or more Storm Maker Nightbane may combine P.P.E. and efforts for a more powerful effect.

areaOfEffect.summary: Base radius of one mile (1.6 km).

duration.summary: Effects take 1D6×10 minutes to manifest, then last one hour; spend the same P.P.E. again each hour to sustain or the weather effect fades.

ranges.summary: Base radius 1 mile (1.6 km); each additional mile doubles the tier P.P.E. cost.

activationTime.summary: Weather effects take 1D6×10 minutes to manifest after P.P.E. is spent.
activationTime.notes: Once manifested, the altered weather lasts one hour before requiring the same P.P.E. spend to sustain.

activationRequirements.summary: Two or more Storm Maker Nightbane may combine their P.P.E. and efforts for a more powerful effect.

rangeAndArea.summary: Base radius 1 mile (1.6 km); each additional mile of radius doubles the tier P.P.E. cost.

rangeMechanics.summary: 1 mile (1.6 km) base radius; each additional mile doubles tier P.P.E. (e.g. moderate change: 10/20/40/80 P.P.E. for 1–4 miles).
rangeMechanics.notes: Area cost doubles per additional mile of radius beyond the first.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 5 P.P.E. — wind ±5 mph (8 km), cloudiness, intensify existing rain, temperature ±4°F.
10 P.P.E. — start rain on overcast day, storm/downpour shift, temperature ±10°F.
20 P.P.E. — rain on clear day, stop downpour, major thunder/electrical storm, temperature ±20°F.
40 P.P.E. — fog, hail/electrical storm on clear day, storm-to-sun, tropical storm (~70 mph/112 km), temperature ±30°F.
80 P.P.E. — hurricane/tornado (90+ mph/144 km), blinding snowstorm, dissipate major storm, temperature ±50°F.

environmentalAmplifiers.summary: Alters rain, wind, and temperature across the affected radius. Multiple Storm Maker Nightbane may pool P.P.E. for greater effect.

combatMechanics.notes: Manifestation delay 1D6×10 minutes. Sustain by spending the same P.P.E. each hour or weather reverts.

statusAlterations.summary: Supernatural weather control across a mile-scale (or larger) area.

name: Switchboard Operator
description: Acts as a supernatural communications hub — a short-range, undetectable telepathic conference link between willing participants that cannot be hacked or jammed by technology.

enhancement.summary: Extend the network.

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. for the first 10 minutes; two P.P.E. per each additional 10 minutes.

cannotAffect: Those shielded by psionic Mind Block or Anti-Magic Cloud
limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot use other Talents while active. The operator is the hub — an open line where all participants hear everything spoken. Up to two additional willing participants per level of experience may join; more than six people plus the operator imposes severe penalties on the operator (-4 initiative, -4 all combat, -2 attacks, -40% skills, Perception bonuses zeroed). Eight or more total participants imposes similar penalties on everyone (-2 initiative, -2 combat, -1 attack, -20% skills). Participants must speak aloud (even a whisper). Only participant voices are transmitted — no background noise. Third-party conversations are one-sided to the link. Telepathy can eavesdrop if the psychic knows the link is active and has access to a participant. Linked participants may move freely within range; only the operator immediately notices a disconnect.

ranges.summary: 1,000 foot (305 m) radius per level of experience.

duration.summary: 10 minutes for 4 P.P.E.; +10 minutes per 2 P.P.E. spent.

activationRequirements.summary: More than six participants plus the operator overloads the hub; eight or more total participants penalizes everyone.

physicalBonusesPenalties.notes: Operator with 7+ total linked (more than six besides self): Perception bonuses zeroed, -4 initiative, -4 all combat, -2 attacks, -40% skills. All participants when 8+ total: Perception bonuses zeroed, -2 initiative, -2 combat, -1 attack, -20% skills.

rangeMechanics.summary: 1,000 foot (305 m) radius per level of experience; linked participants may move freely within range.

passengerCompanionCapacity.summary: Operator plus up to two additional willing participants per level of experience.
passengerCompanionCapacity.notes: Willing participants only. Operator immediately notices disconnects; participants may leave at any time while remaining in range.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Cannot use other Talents while Switchboard Operator is active. Operator overload penalties apply when more than six participants besides self are linked.

statusAlterations.summary: Supernatural telepathic conference network — undetectable and unhackable by technology; open line where all participants hear everything spoken.

combatMechanics.notes: Participants must speak aloud (even a whisper). Only participant voices transmit — no background noise. Third-party conversations are one-sided to the link. Telepathy can eavesdrop if the psychic knows the link is active and has access to a participant. Mind Block and Anti-Magic Cloud block connection.

name: The Shroud
description: Becomes invisible and noiseless to normal vision, senses, and electronic devices (cameras, motion detectors, heat sensors, etc.). Can be used selectively so some observers still see the Nightbane. Very expensive to maintain (10 P.P.E. per minute). Attacking ends the effect immediately — threatening, screaming, or gesturing does not.

enhancement.summary: 10 P.P.E. per each minute in effect.

ppe.notes: 10 P.P.E. to activate; 10 P.P.E. per each minute the Shroud is maintained.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot activate while in plain sight or actively engaged in combat — must get out of sight first. Any attack ends invisibility for all observers; threats, screams, and gestures are allowed.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: 10 P.P.E. per minute in effect.

activationRequirements.summary: Cannot activate while in plain sight or actively engaged in combat — must get out of sight first. Attacking anyone ends the Shroud immediately; threatening, screaming, and gesturing are allowed.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Out of sight to activate; no attacks while shrouded

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — selective invisibility to chosen observers.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Invisible and noiseless to most observers and electronics while chosen individuals may still see the Nightbane.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Invisible and noiseless to normal vision, senses, and electronic devices (cameras, motion detectors, heat sensors, etc.). May be used to haunt a single visible victim while remaining unseen to everyone else.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Any attack ends the Shroud for all observers. Cannot reactivate while in plain sight or actively fighting — must break line of sight first.

combatMechanics.notes: Threats, screams, and gestures do not break the Shroud — only making an attack does. Electronic surveillance is fooled while active.

statusAlterations.summary: Invisible and noiseless to normal senses and electronic detection; selectively visible to chosen observers.

statusEffects.name: The Shroud
statusEffects.summary: Undetectable to normal vision, hearing, and electronic sensors unless the user allows a specific observer to see them.

name: Trap Shadow
description: Converts a patch of shadow into a programmable booby-trap — impaling spikes, grabbing tendrils, or crushing darkness that activates on set criteria or at the creator's command.

ppe.notes: Seven P.P.E. per Trap Shadow created.

limitations.otherLimitations: Requires a shadow or patch of darkness at least 5 square feet (0.46 m²). Affects one target per level of experience per trigger. Trap Shadows are mentally programmed (species, headcount, proximity, etc.) and may be triggered at will by the creator with line of sight. Victims dragged into the trap are heavily cloaked, barely visible, barely audible, temporarily removed from play, and cannot be attacked while trapped (must come within 4 feet / 1.2 m of the shadow). Damage is supernatural — minor welts remain on humans; those killed appear to have died from cardiac arrest. Re-entering the shadow or new enemies can retrigger it. Strong bright light (more than a flashlight) destroys the trap after 1D6+1 minutes of exposure.

ranges.summary: Reach: 1 foot (0.3 m) radius per level. Remote trigger: up to 100 feet (30.5 m) per level (line of sight).
ranges.distanceValue: 1 foot reach per level; 100 feet remote trigger per level

duration.summary: Set trap lasts until triggered or 10 minutes per level; hold lasts 1 melee round per level on failed save (1 melee round on successful save).

save.summary: 15 or higher (including P.E. bonus). Failure: 3D6 damage and immobilized 1 melee round per creator level. Success: half damage, trapped 1 melee round (15 seconds).

notes: After the hold expires, victims are expelled from the shadow. Trap may be left behind when set.

activationRequirements.summary: Requires shadow or darkness at least 5 square feet (0.46 m²). Trap is mentally programmed (species, headcount, proximity, etc.). Remote activation requires line of sight. One target per creator level per trigger.

rangeMechanics.summary: Reach: 1 foot (0.3 m) radius per level to grab; remote trigger up to 100 feet (30.5 m) per level with line of sight.
rangeMechanics.notes: Victims must come within 4 feet (1.2 m) of the shadow to be pulled in.

damage.summary: 3D6 supernatural damage on failed save; half on success.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: 3D6 damage and immobilized 1 melee round per creator level.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Half damage; trapped 1 melee round (15 seconds).
savingThrow.notes: Include P.E. bonus to save.

combatMechanics.notes: Damage is not physical — humans show only minor welts; kills appear as cardiac arrest. Strong bright light destroys trap after 1D6+1 minutes.

holdsAndEscapes.summary: Failed save: immobilized in shadow 1 melee round per creator level. Success: 1 melee round hold. Trapped victims are removed from play and cannot be attacked while inside.
holdsAndEscapes.holdDurationSummary: 1 melee round per creator level (failed save) or 1 melee round (successful save).

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Victims dragged in are heavily cloaked — barely visible and barely audible; temporarily removed from play.

environmentalConstructs.summary: Trap may be set and left behind; lasts until triggered or 10 minutes per creator level.

statusAlterations.summary: Shadow converts to programmable booby-trap — spikes, tendrils, or crushing darkness.

damageModifiersNote: Supernatural damage only; re-entry or new enemies retrigger the trap.

name: Ultra-Vision
description: Grants multi-spectrum sight plus telescopic (4–8×30 magnification) and microscopic (2–20×) vision — useful for spotting laser trip wires, invisible inks, physical traces, and limited X-ray sight through low-density non-metallic materials.

enhancement.summary: Extend sight duration.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. for the first two minutes; one P.P.E. per each subsequent two minutes.

limitations.otherLimitations: Electromagnetic frequencies in the microwave range and lower are not visible, nor are high-powered X-rays or above.

ranges.summary: 2 feet (0.6 m) for microscopic and X-ray vision; standard visual range for infrared and ultraviolet; up to 2 miles (3.2 km) for telescopic vision.

duration.summary: Two minutes base; +2 minutes per 1 P.P.E. spent after activation.

notes: Originally developed by Nightbane working with the Lightbringer faction.

rangeMechanics.summary: 2 feet (0.6 m) for microscopic and X-ray; standard visual range for infrared and ultraviolet; up to 2 miles (3.2 km) telescopic.
rangeMechanics.notes: Telescopic sight up to 2 miles (3.2 km).

activationRequirements.summary: Microwave-range electromagnetic frequencies and lower are not visible; high-powered X-rays and above are not visible.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Telescopic (4–8×30 magnification), microscopic (2–20×), infrared/ultraviolet, and limited X-ray through low-density non-metallic materials (cloth, tents, flesh). Spots laser trip wires, targeting lasers, invisible inks, and physical traces.

statusAlterations.summary: Multi-spectrum enhanced sight for investigation and espionage.

name: Wall Runner
description: Defies gravity to run up or across vertical surfaces as if they were flat ground — speed, carrying capacity, and combat ability are unaffected.

enhancement.summary: Extend wall-running duration.

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. for the first two minutes; two P.P.E. per each additional minute.

limitations.otherLimitations: Most skyscraper windows and glass sides support a runner's weight; smaller buildings and homes usually do not (G.M. discretion). Losing contact with the vertical surface ends the effect and the user falls. Falls from four stories or more are likely deadly.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Two minutes base; +1 minute per 2 P.P.E. spent after activation.

notes: Opponents fighting the Wall Runner at a 90-degree angle suffer -3 to strike, parry, and dodge due to the unusual angles of attack.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — vertical surfaces treated as flat ground.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Defies gravity to run up or across vertical surfaces; speed and carrying capacity unaffected.
mobilityBlocks.notes: Losing contact with the surface ends the effect and causes a fall.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: -3 to strike, parry, and dodge against the Wall Runner due to unusual attack angles.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Losing contact with the vertical surface ends gravity defiance — user falls. Falls from four stories or more are likely deadly. Most skyscraper glass supports weight; smaller building windows usually do not (G.M. discretion).

combatMechanics.notes: Combat ability unaffected while wall-running. Opponents fighting at a 90-degree angle suffer -3 strike, parry, and dodge.

statusAlterations.summary: Urban vertical mobility — treats walls and ceilings as traversable ground.

name: Wall Whisperer
description: Reads psychic imprints left on a wall or building section — witnessing past events from construction onward via specific times, scrolling timelines, or emotionally charged occurrences nearby.

ppe.notes: Eight P.P.E. per activation.

cannotAffect: Areas protected by the Sanctum spell
limitations.otherLimitations: Unavailable until 2nd level. Touch the wall to activate. Images and sound only — no smell or touch sensation; details may be sketchy. Conversations beyond 4 feet (1.2 m) from the wall may yield only snippets; foreign languages are heard as spoken. Momentous or traumatic events may force a save vs psionic attack (17+) or the user must relive them. Review takes 1D6 minutes for a known exact time/event, otherwise hours of sampling; the user enters a trance and may lose track of time. Careful viewing takes as long as the original events.

ranges.summary: Touch to activate. 20 feet (6.1 m) on both sides of a 20-foot wall length, +10 feet (3 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Maximum one hour to review memories per activation.

save.summary: Save vs psionic attack 17 or better to avoid being forced to relive highly traumatic wall memories.

notes: Similar to Object Read but scoped to an entire wall section. Perspective can be anywhere on the chosen wall, including glimpses through windows.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Call up events at a specific moment — 1D6 minutes to review if the time or event is known.
Start at a date/time and scan forward or backward for a person, event, or notable occurrence.
Open to highly emotional occurrences within 20 feet (6.1 m) of the wall — strongest impressions surface first.

activationRequirements.summary: Touch the chosen wall to activate. Sanctum and similar measures can block use.

rangeMechanics.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) on both sides of a 20-foot wall length, +10 feet (3 m) per level of experience.

activationTime.summary: 1D6 minutes for a known exact time/event; otherwise hours of sampling. Maximum one hour to review per activation.
activationTime.notes: User enters a trance; careful viewing takes as long as the original events.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Forced to relive momentous or traumatic wall memories.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: May choose whether to relive traumatic impressions.

combatMechanics.notes: Images and sound only — no smell or touch. Details may be sketchy; conversations beyond 4 feet (1.2 m) from the wall may yield only snippets.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Enters a trance during review and may lose track of time.

statusAlterations.summary: Reads psychic imprints on a wall section from construction onward — similar to Object Read at building scale.

name: Witch-Hunter's Sight
description: Reveals P.P.E. as a bluish glow on beings and objects with significant reserves — spotting disguised supernaturals, magic users, Nightbane, magical items, and ley lines or nexuses even in daylight.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only registers beings or objects with more than 30 P.P.E.; those with less than 12 P.P.E. do not register. Smoke, mist, and thin cloth or cardboard do not hide the glow, but it does not shine through walls, doors, heavy crates, or similar barriers. While active: -3 to all Perception Rolls (focused on high-P.P.E. targets). On a ley line or at a nexus: -6 to Perception Rolls involving vision. Vs a target with 500+ P.P.E.: -4 to all combat rolls against that being. Vs 1,000+ P.P.E.: -8 to all combat rolls (blinding). Penalties end when the Talent expires or is canceled.

ranges.summary: Equal to the Nightbane's natural nightvision range.

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience.

save.summary: +4 to save vs illusion while active.

notes: With practice, can estimate a target's P.P.E. reserve within ±1D4×10%.

rangeMechanics.summary: Equal to the Nightbane's natural nightvision range.
rangeMechanics.notes: Glow visible through smoke, mist, thin cloth, and cardboard — not through walls, doors, or heavy barriers.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Sees P.P.E. as bluish glow on beings/objects with 30+ P.P.E. Ley lines and nexuses visible in daylight. +4 to save vs illusion. With practice, estimates reserves within ±1D4×10%.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: -3 to all Perception Rolls while active. On a ley line or at a nexus: -6 to Perception Rolls involving vision. Vs a target with 500+ P.P.E.: -4 to all combat rolls. Vs 1,000+ P.P.E.: -8 to all combat rolls (blinding). Penalties end when the Talent expires or is canceled.

savingThrow.notes: +4 to save vs illusion while active.

combatMechanics.notes: Beings or objects under 12 P.P.E. do not register; only 30+ P.P.E. appears as significant glow.

statusAlterations.summary: Perceives magical energy as bluish radiance — useful for hunting disguised supernaturals, magic users, and magical items.
